    48→parse_variable <- function(var, design) {
    49→  ret <- if (purrr::is_formula(var)) {
    50→    vnames <- all.vars(var[[2]])
    51→    ret <- if (length(vnames) > 1) {
    52→      do.call("interaction", c(lapply(vnames, function(vname) as.factor(design[[vname]])), sep=":"))
    53→    } else {
    54→      design[[vnames]]
    55→    }
    56→    
    57→    assertthat::assert_that(!is.null(ret), msg=paste("formula variable", 
    58→                                                     paste(as.character(var), collapse=" "), " not found."))
    59→    ret
    60→    
    61→  } else if (is.character(var) && length(var) == 1) {
    62→    if (is.null(design[[var]])) {
    63→      stop(paste("`design` does not contain variable named: ", var))
    64→    }
    65→    design[[var]]
    66→  } else if (is.factor(var) || is.integer(var)) {
    67→    if (is.data.frame(design)) {
    68→      assertthat::assert_that(nrow(design) == length(var)) 
    69→    }
    70→    var
    71→  } else {
    72→    stop("'var' must be a formula, factor, or character vector")
    73→  }
    74→  
    75→  if (is.factor(ret)) {
    76→    droplevels(ret)
    77→  } else {
    78→    ret
    79→  }

    81→}

    84→#' Create an MVPA Design Object
    85→#'
    86→#' Creates a design object for MVPA analysis that encapsulates training and testing designs,
    87→#' response variables, and optional blocking and splitting factors.
    88→#'
    89→#' @param train_design A data frame containing the training design matrix
    90→#' @param test_design Optional data frame containing the test design matrix (default: NULL)
    91→#' @param y_train Formula or vector specifying the training response variable
    92→#' @param y_test Optional formula or vector specifying the test response variable (default: NULL)
    93→#' @param block_var Optional formula or vector specifying the blocking variable for cross-validation
    94→#' @param split_by Optional formula or vector for splitting analyses
    95→#' @param ... Additional arguments (currently unused)
    96→#'
    97→#' @return An \code{mvpa_design} object (S3 class) containing:
    98→#'   \describe{
    99→#'     \item{train_design}{Data frame of training design}
   100→#'     \item{test_design}{Data frame of test design (if provided)}
   101→#'     \item{y_train}{Training response variable}
   102→#'     \item{y_test}{Test response variable (if provided)}
   103→#'     \item{block_var}{Blocking variable for cross-validation (if provided)}
   104→#'     \item{split_by}{Splitting factor (if provided)}
   105→#'   }
   106→#'
   107→#' @details
   108→#' The \code{y_train} and \code{y_test} can be specified either as formulas (e.g., ~ condition) 
   109→#' or as vectors. If formulas are used, they are evaluated within the respective design matrices.
   110→#' 
   111→#' The \code{block_var} and \code{split_by} can also be specified as formulas or vectors. 
   112→#' If formulas, they are evaluated within the training design matrix.
   113→#'
   114→#' @examples
   115→#' # Basic design with only training data
   116→#' train_df <- data.frame(condition = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 50),
   117→#'                        block = rep(1:5, each = 20),
   118→#'                        group = rep(c("Group1", "Group2"), 50))
   119→#' design <- mvpa_design(train_df, y_train = ~ condition)
   120→#'
   121→#' # Design with test data and blocking variable
   122→#' test_df <- data.frame(condition = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 25))
   123→#' design_with_test <- mvpa_design(
   124→#'   train_df, 
   125→#'   test_df, 
   126→#'   y_train = ~ condition, 
   127→#'   y_test = ~ condition,
   128→#'   block_var = ~ block
   129→#' )
   130→#'
   131→#' # Design with split_by factor
   132→#' design_split <- mvpa_design(
   133→#'   train_df, 
   134→#'   y_train = ~ condition,
   135→#'   split_by = ~ group
   136→#' )
   137→#'
   138→#' @seealso 
   139→#' \code{\link{mvpa_dataset}} for creating the corresponding dataset object
   140→#'
   141→#' @importFrom stats as.formula
   142→#' @export
   143→mvpa_design <- function(train_design, test_design=NULL, y_train, y_test=NULL, block_var=NULL, split_by=NULL, ...) {
   144→ 
   145→  y_train <- if (!purrr::is_formula(y_train) && length(y_train) > 1) {
   146→    y_train
   147→  } else {
   148→    parse_variable(y_train, train_design)
   149→  }

for use in MVPA analyses.
"""

import sys
sys.path.append('/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/pyneuroim/src')
from pyneuroim import NeuroVec, NeuroVol, DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol


class MVPADataset:
    """MVPA Dataset for volumetric neuroimaging data.
    
    This class encapsulates training data, optional test data, and a voxel mask
    for MVPA analysis. It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_dataset.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    train_data : NeuroVec
        The training data as a 4D neuroimaging vector (x, y, z, time)
    test_data : NeuroVec, optional
        The test data as a 4D neuroimaging vector
    mask : NeuroVol
        Binary mask defining valid voxels for analysis
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    train_data : NeuroVec
        The training data
    test_data : NeuroVec or None
        The test data if provided
    mask : NeuroVol
        The binary mask
    has_test_set : bool
        Whether this dataset has a test set
        
    Raises
    ------
    TypeError
        If inputs are not of correct type
    ValueError
        If mask has only 1 or fewer active voxels
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> # Create dataset from NeuroVec objects
    >>> from pyneuroim import DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol, NeuroSpace
    >>> space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 100])
    >>> train_vec = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 100), space)
    >>> mask_vol = DenseNeuroVol(np.ones((10, 10, 10)), space.drop_dim(3))
    >>> dataset = MVPADataset(train_vec, mask=mask_vol)
    """
    
    def __init__(self, train_data: NeuroVec, test_data: Optional[NeuroVec] = None, 
                 mask: NeuroVol = None):
        # Type checking
        if not isinstance(train_data, NeuroVec):
            raise TypeError("train_data must be a NeuroVec instance")
        
        if test_data is not None and not isinstance(test_data, NeuroVec):
            raise TypeError("test_data must be a NeuroVec instance")
            
        if not isinstance(mask, NeuroVol):
            raise TypeError("mask must be a NeuroVol instance")
        
        # Check for single-voxel datasets
        mask_dims = mask.shape[:3]
        total_voxels = np.prod(mask_dims)
        if total_voxels <= 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid dataset: Only 1 voxel detected (dimensions "
                           f"{mask_dims[0]}×{mask_dims[1]}×{mask_dims[2]}). "
                           f"Feature RSA analysis requires multiple voxels.")
        
        # Check for active voxels in mask
        if hasattr(mask, 'data'):
            active_voxels = np.sum(mask.data > 0)
        else:
            # For sparse or other mask types
            active_voxels = np.sum(np.asarray(mask) > 0)
            
        if active_voxels <= 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid dataset: Only {active_voxels} active voxel(s) in mask. "
                           f"Feature RSA analysis requires multiple active voxels.")
        
        self.train_data = train_data
        self.test_data = test_data
        self.mask = mask
        self.has_test_set = test_data is not None
        
    @property
    def n_timepoints(self) -> int:
        """Number of timepoints in training data."""
        return self.train_data.shape[3]
    
    @property
    def n_voxels(self) -> int:
        """Number of active voxels in mask."""
        if hasattr(self.mask, 'data'):
            return int(np.sum(self.mask.data > 0))
        else:
            return int(np.sum(np.asarray(self.mask) > 0))
    
    @property
    def mask_indices(self) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices of active voxels in mask."""
        if hasattr(self.mask, 'data'):
            return np.where(self.mask.data > 0)
        else:
            return np.where(np.asarray(self.mask) > 0)
    
    def get_masked_data(self, data: Optional[NeuroVec] = None) -> np.ndarray:
        """Extract masked data as 2D array (timepoints x voxels).
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        data : NeuroVec, optional
            Data to mask. If None, uses train_data
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            2D array of shape (n_timepoints, n_voxels)
        """
        if data is None:
            data = self.train_data
            
        # Get mask indices
        mask_idx = self.mask_indices
        
        # Extract data at mask locations
        if hasattr(data, 'data'):
            # Dense data
            masked = data.data[mask_idx[0], mask_idx[1], mask_idx[2], :].T
        else:
            # Handle other data types
            masked = data[mask_idx[0], mask_idx[1], mask_idx[2], :].T
            
        return masked
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"MVPADataset\n"
                f"  Training samples : {self.n_timepoints}\n"
                f"  Active voxels    : {self.n_voxels}\n"
                f"  Has test set     : {self.has_test_set}\n"
                f"  Dimensions       : {' × '.join(map(str, self.train_data.shape[:3]))}")


class MVPASurfaceDataset:
    """MVPA Dataset for surface-based neuroimaging data.
    
    This class encapsulates training data, optional test data, and a vertex mask
    for surface-based MVPA analysis. It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_surface_dataset.
    
    Note: This is a placeholder implementation until neurosurf is ported to Python.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    train_data : object
        The training data (placeholder for NeuroSurfaceVector)
    test_data : object, optional
        The test data (placeholder for NeuroSurfaceVector)
    mask : np.ndarray, optional
        Binary mask for vertices. If None, creates from training data
    name : str, optional
        Label to identify the dataset (e.g., "lh" or "rh")
        
    Raises
    ------
    NotImplementedError
        This class requires the neurosurf package to be ported
    """
    
    def __init__(self, train_data, test_data=None, mask=None, name=""):
        raise NotImplementedError(
            "MVPASurfaceDataset requires the neurosurf package to be ported to Python. "
            "Please use MVPADataset for volumetric data instead."
        )


def gen_sample_dataset(D: Union[Tuple[int, ...], int], 
                      nobs: int,
                      response_type: str = "categorical",
                      data_mode: str = "image", 
                      spacing: Tuple[float, float, float] = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
                      blocks: int = 5,
                      nlevels: int = 5,
                      external_test: bool = False,
                      ntest_obs: Optional[int] = None,
                      split_by: Optional[np.ndarray] = None,
                      na_cols: int = 0) -> dict:
    """Generate sample dataset for MVPA analysis.
    
    Creates a synthetic dataset for testing and demonstration of MVPA analyses.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    D : tuple or int
        Data dimensions: tuple of length 3 for image data, or single number for surface data
    nobs : int
        Number of observations
    response_type : {'categorical', 'continuous'}
        Type of response variable
    data_mode : {'image', 'surface'}
        Type of data to generate
    spacing : tuple
        Voxel spacing (default: (1, 1, 1))
    blocks : int
        Number of blocks for cross-validation
    nlevels : int
        Number of category levels (for categorical response)
    external_test : bool
        Whether to generate an external test set
    ntest_obs : int, optional
        Number of test observations (default: same as nobs)
    split_by : array-like, optional
        Factor for splitting analyses
    na_cols : int
        Number of columns to randomly set to NA
        
    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary with 'dataset' (MVPADataset) and 'design' (MVPADesign)
    """
    if data_mode == "surface":
        raise NotImplementedError("Surface data generation requires neurosurf package")
    
    if ntest_obs is None:
        ntest_obs = nobs
    
    # Import MVPADesign here to avoid circular import
    from .design import MVPADesign
    
    # Create synthetic image data
    if isinstance(D, int):
        D = (D,)
    
    # Generate random data
    from pyneuroim import NeuroSpace, DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol
    
    # Training data
    mat = np.random.randn(*D, nobs)
    
    # Add NA columns if requested
    if na_cols > 0:
        na_idx = np.random.choice(np.prod(D[:3]), na_cols, replace=False)
        for idx in na_idx:
            i, j, k = np.unravel_index(idx, D[:3])
            mat[i, j, k, :] = np.nan
    
    bspace = NeuroSpace(dim=D + (nobs,), spacing=spacing + (1,))
    bvec = DenseNeuroVec(mat, bspace)
    
    # Create spherical mask
    mask_space = NeuroSpace(dim=D, spacing=spacing)
    mask_data = np.zeros(D)
    
    # Create spherical ROI in center
    center = np.array([d//2 for d in D])
    radius = min(D) // 2
    
    for i in range(D[0]):
        for j in range(D[1]):
            for k in range(D[2]):
                if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= radius:
                    mask_data[i, j, k] = 1
    
    mask = DenseNeuroVol(mask_data, mask_space)
    
    # Create test data if requested
    if external_test:
        test_mat = np.random.randn(*D, ntest_obs)
        test_space = NeuroSpace(dim=D + (ntest_obs,), spacing=spacing + (1,))
        test_vec = DenseNeuroVec(test_mat, test_space)
        dset = MVPADataset(train_data=bvec, test_data=test_vec, mask=mask)
    else:
        dset = MVPADataset(train_data=bvec, mask=mask)
    
    # Create design
    if response_type == "categorical":
        y_train = np.repeat(np.arange(nlevels), nobs // nlevels + 1)[:nobs]
        y_train = np.array([f"level_{i}" for i in y_train])
        if external_test:
            y_test = np.repeat(np.arange(nlevels), ntest_obs // nlevels + 1)[:ntest_obs]
            y_test = np.array([f"level_{i}" for i in y_test])
        else:
            y_test = None
    else:
        y_train = np.random.randn(nobs)
        if external_test:
            y_test = np.random.randn(ntest_obs)
        else:
            y_test = None
    
    # Create block variable
    block_var = np.repeat(np.arange(blocks), nobs // blocks + 1)[:nobs]
    
    # Create design object
    design = MVPADesign(
        y_train=y_train,
        y_test=y_test,
        block_var=block_var,
        split_by=split_by
    )
    
    return {"dataset": dset, "design": design}

"""MVPA Dataset classes for volumetric and surface-based neuroimaging data.

This module provides dataset containers that wrap neuroimaging data
for use in MVPA analyses.
"""

from typing import Optional, Union, Tuple
import numpy as np
import warnings

# Import from pyneuroim

from pyneuroim import NeuroVec, NeuroVol, DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol


class MVPADataset:
    """MVPA Dataset for volumetric neuroimaging data.
    
    This class encapsulates training data, optional test data, and a voxel mask
    for MVPA analysis. It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_dataset.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    train_data : NeuroVec
        The training data as a 4D neuroimaging vector (x, y, z, time)
    test_data : NeuroVec, optional
        The test data as a 4D neuroimaging vector
    mask : NeuroVol
        Binary mask defining valid voxels for analysis
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    train_data : NeuroVec
        The training data
    test_data : NeuroVec or None
        The test data if provided
    mask : NeuroVol
        The binary mask
    has_test_set : bool
        Whether this dataset has a test set
        
    Raises
    ------
    TypeError
        If inputs are not of correct type
    ValueError
        If mask has only 1 or fewer active voxels
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> # Create dataset from NeuroVec objects
    >>> from pyneuroim import DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol, NeuroSpace
    >>> space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 100])
    >>> train_vec = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 100), space)
    >>> mask_vol = DenseNeuroVol(np.ones((10, 10, 10)), space.drop_dim(3))
    >>> dataset = MVPADataset(train_vec, mask=mask_vol)
    """
    
    def __init__(self, train_data: NeuroVec, test_data: Optional[NeuroVec] = None, 
                 mask: NeuroVol = None):
        # Type checking
        if not isinstance(train_data, NeuroVec):
            raise TypeError("train_data must be a NeuroVec instance")
        
        if test_data is not None and not isinstance(test_data, NeuroVec):
            raise TypeError("test_data must be a NeuroVec instance")
            
        if not isinstance(mask, NeuroVol):
            raise TypeError("mask must be a NeuroVol instance")
        
        # Check for single-voxel datasets
        mask_dims = mask.shape[:3]
        total_voxels = np.prod(mask_dims)
        if total_voxels <= 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid dataset: Only 1 voxel detected (dimensions "
                           f"{mask_dims[0]}×{mask_dims[1]}×{mask_dims[2]}). "
                           f"Feature RSA analysis requires multiple voxels.")
        
        # Check for active voxels in mask
        if hasattr(mask, 'data'):
            active_voxels = np.sum(mask.data > 0)
        else:
            # For sparse or other mask types
            active_voxels = np.sum(np.asarray(mask) > 0)
            
        if active_voxels <= 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid dataset: Only {active_voxels} active voxel(s) in mask. "
                           f"Feature RSA analysis requires multiple active voxels.")
        
        self.train_data = train_data
        self.test_data = test_data
        self.mask = mask
        self.has_test_set = test_data is not None
        
    @property
    def n_timepoints(self) -> int:
        """Number of timepoints in training data."""
        return self.train_data.shape[3]
    
    @property
    def n_voxels(self) -> int:
        """Number of active voxels in mask."""
        if hasattr(self.mask, 'data'):
            return int(np.sum(self.mask.data > 0))
        else:
            return int(np.sum(np.asarray(self.mask) > 0))
    
    @property
    def mask_indices(self) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices of active voxels in mask."""
        if hasattr(self.mask, 'data'):
            return np.where(self.mask.data > 0)
        else:
            return np.where(np.asarray(self.mask) > 0)
    
    def get_masked_data(self, data: Optional[NeuroVec] = None) -> np.ndarray:
        """Extract masked data as 2D array (timepoints x voxels).
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        data : NeuroVec, optional
            Data to mask. If None, uses train_data
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            2D array of shape (n_timepoints, n_voxels)
        """
        if data is None:
            data = self.train_data
            
        # Get mask indices
        mask_idx = self.mask_indices
        
        # Extract data at mask locations
        if hasattr(data, 'data'):
            # Dense data
            masked = data.data[mask_idx[0], mask_idx[1], mask_idx[2], :].T
        else:
            # Handle other data types
            masked = data[mask_idx[0], mask_idx[1], mask_idx[2], :].T
            
        return masked
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"MVPADataset\n"
                f"  Training samples : {self.n_timepoints}\n"
                f"  Active voxels    : {self.n_voxels}\n"
                f"  Has test set     : {self.has_test_set}\n"
                f"  Dimensions       : {' × '.join(map(str, self.train_data.shape[:3]))}")

                      external_test: bool = False,
                      ntest_obs: Optional[int] = None,
                      split_by: Optional[np.ndarray] = None,
                      na_cols: int = 0) -> dict:

    nobs : int
        Number of observations
    response_type : {'categorical', 'continuous'}
        Type of response variable

        Type of data to generate
    spacing : tuple
        Voxel spacing (default: (1, 1, 1))
    blocks : int
        Number of blocks for cross-validation
    nlevels : int
        Number of category levels (for categorical response)
    external_test : bool
        Whether to generate an external test set
    ntest_obs : int, optional
        Number of test observations (default: same as nobs)
    split_by : array-like, optional
        Factor for splitting analyses
    na_cols : int
        Number of columns to randomly set to NA
        
    Returns
    -------

        Dictionary with 'dataset' (MVPADataset) and 'design' (MVPADesign)
    """
    if data_mode == "surface":
        raise NotImplementedError("Surface data generation requires neurosurf package")
    
    if ntest_obs is None:

    from pyneuroim import NeuroSpace, DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol
    
    # Training data
    mat = np.random.randn(*D, nobs)

    if na_cols > 0:
        na_idx = np.random.choice(np.prod(D[:3]), na_cols, replace=False)
        for idx in na_idx:
            i, j, k = np.unravel_index(idx, D[:3])
            mat[i, j, k, :] = np.nan
    
    bspace = NeuroSpace(dim=D + (nobs,), spacing=spacing + (1,))

    center = np.array([d//2 for d in D])
    radius = min(D) // 2
    
    for i in range(D[0]):
        for j in range(D[1]):
            for k in range(D[2]):
                if np.sqrt((i-center[0])**2 + (j-center[1])**2 + (k-center[2])**2) <= radius:
                    mask_data[i, j, k] = 1
    
    mask = DenseNeuroVol(mask_data, mask_space)
    
    # Create test data if requested
    if external_test:
        test_mat = np.random.randn(*D, ntest_obs)
        test_space = NeuroSpace(dim=D + (ntest_obs,), spacing=spacing + (1,))
        test_vec = DenseNeuroVec(test_mat, test_space)
        dset = MVPADataset(train_data=bvec, test_data=test_vec, mask=mask)
    else:
        dset = MVPADataset(train_data=bvec, mask=mask)
    
    # Create design
    if response_type == "categorical":
        y_train = np.repeat(np.arange(nlevels), nobs // nlevels + 1)[:nobs]
        y_train = np.array([f"level_{i}" for i in y_train])
        if external_test:

"""MVPA Design class for experimental design specification.

This module provides the MVPADesign class that encapsulates experimental
design information including response variables, blocking factors, and
splitting variables.
"""

from typing import Optional, Union, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from dataclasses import dataclass


def parse_variable(var: Union[str, np.ndarray], 
                  design: Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]) -> np.ndarray:
    """Parse a variable specification from design matrix.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    var : str or array-like
        Variable specification - either column name or array
    design : DataFrame or dict
        Design matrix containing variables
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        The parsed variable
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If variable not found in design
    """
    if isinstance(var, str):
        if isinstance(design, pd.DataFrame):
            if var not in design.columns:
                raise ValueError(f"Design does not contain variable named: {var}")
            return design[var].values
        else:
            if var not in design:
                raise ValueError(f"Design does not contain variable named: {var}")
            return design[var]
    elif isinstance(var, (np.ndarray, list)):
        return np.asarray(var)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Variable must be a string name or array-like")


@dataclass
class MVPADesign:
    """MVPA Design object for experimental design specification.
    
    This class encapsulates training and testing designs, response variables,
    and optional blocking and splitting factors. It is the Python equivalent
    of R's mvpa_design.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    y_train : array-like
        Training response variable (labels or continuous values)
    y_test : array-like, optional
        Test response variable
    train_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Training design matrix with additional covariates
    test_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Test design matrix
    block_var : array-like, optional
        Blocking variable for cross-validation
    split_by : array-like, optional
        Variable for splitting analyses
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    y_train : np.ndarray
        Training response variable
    y_test : np.ndarray or None
        Test response variable
    train_design : DataFrame or dict or None
        Training design matrix
    test_design : DataFrame or dict or None
        Test design matrix
    block_var : np.ndarray or None
        Blocking variable
    split_by : np.ndarray or None
        Splitting variable
    has_test_set : bool
        Whether design includes test set
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> # Basic design with only response variable
    >>> y = np.array(['A', 'B', 'A', 'B', 'A', 'B'])
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=y)
    
    >>> # Design with blocking variable
    >>> blocks = np.array([1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3])
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=y, block_var=blocks)
    
    >>> # Design with full design matrix
    >>> df = pd.DataFrame({
    ...     'condition': ['A', 'B', 'A', 'B'],
    ...     'subject': [1, 1, 2, 2]
    ... })
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=df['condition'], train_design=df)
    """
    
    y_train: np.ndarray
    y_test: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    train_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None
    test_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None
    block_var: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    split_by: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        """Convert inputs to numpy arrays and validate."""
        # Ensure y_train is numpy array
        self.y_train = np.asarray(self.y_train)
        
        # Convert other variables if provided
        if self.y_test is not None:
            self.y_test = np.asarray(self.y_test)
            
        if self.block_var is not None:
            self.block_var = np.asarray(self.block_var)
            # Validate length
            if len(self.block_var) != len(self.y_train):
                raise ValueError(f"block_var length ({len(self.block_var)}) must match "
                               f"y_train length ({len(self.y_train)})")
        
        if self.split_by is not None:
            self.split_by = np.asarray(self.split_by)
            # Validate length
            if len(self.split_by) != len(self.y_train):
                raise ValueError(f"split_by length ({len(self.split_by)}) must match "
                               f"y_train length ({len(self.y_train)})")
    
    @property
    def has_test_set(self) -> bool:
        """Whether this design has a test set."""
        return self.y_test is not None
    
    @property
    def n_train(self) -> int:
        """Number of training observations."""
        return len(self.y_train)
    
    @property
    def n_test(self) -> int:
        """Number of test observations."""
        return 0 if self.y_test is None else len(self.y_test)
    
    @property
    def is_classification(self) -> bool:
        """Whether this is a classification problem."""
        # Check if y_train contains strings or has few unique values
        if self.y_train.dtype.kind in {'U', 'S', 'O'}:
            return True
        unique_vals = np.unique(self.y_train)
        # If numeric with few unique values, likely classification
        return len(unique_vals) < 0.1 * len(self.y_train)
    
    @property
    def n_classes(self) -> Optional[int]:
        """Number of classes for classification problems."""
        if self.is_classification:
            return len(np.unique(self.y_train))
        return None
    
    @property
    def classes(self) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
        """Unique class labels for classification problems."""
        if self.is_classification:
            return np.unique(self.y_train)
        return None
    
    def get_train_variable(self, name: str) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get variable from training design.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Variable name
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Variable values
        """
        if self.train_design is None:
            raise ValueError("No training design matrix available")
        return parse_variable(name, self.train_design)
    
    def get_test_variable(self, name: str) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get variable from test design.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Variable name
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Variable values
        """
        if self.test_design is None:
            raise ValueError("No test design matrix available")
        return parse_variable(name, self.test_design)
    
    def split_indices(self) -> Dict[Any, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices for each split group.
        
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Mapping from split values to indices
        """
        if self.split_by is None:
            raise ValueError("No split_by variable defined")
            
        indices = {}
        for val in np.unique(self.split_by):
            indices[val] = np.where(self.split_by == val)[0]
        return indices
    
    def block_indices(self) -> Dict[Any, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices for each block.
        
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Mapping from block values to indices
        """
        if self.block_var is None:
            raise ValueError("No block_var defined")
            
        indices = {}
        for val in np.unique(self.block_var):
            indices[val] = np.where(self.block_var == val)[0]
        return indices
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        lines = ["MVPADesign"]
        lines.append(f"  Training samples : {self.n_train}")
        if self.has_test_set:
            lines.append(f"  Test samples     : {self.n_test}")
        
        if self.is_classification:
            lines.append(f"  Type             : Classification ({self.n_classes} classes)")
            lines.append(f"  Classes          : {self.classes}")
        else:
            lines.append(f"  Type             : Regression")
            
        if self.block_var is not None:
            n_blocks = len(np.unique(self.block_var))
            lines.append(f"  Blocks           : {n_blocks}")
            
        if self.split_by is not None:
            n_splits = len(np.unique(self.split_by))
            lines.append(f"  Split groups     : {n_splits}")
            
        return "\n".join(lines)


def mvpa_design(train_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None,
                test_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None,
                y_train: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                y_test: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                block_var: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                split_by: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                **kwargs) -> MVPADesign:
    """Create an MVPA Design object.
    
    Convenience function that handles variable parsing from design matrices.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    train_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Training design matrix
    test_design : DataFrame or dict, optional  
        Test design matrix
    y_train : str or array-like
        Training response - column name or array
    y_test : str or array-like, optional
        Test response - column name or array
    block_var : str or array-like, optional
        Blocking variable - column name or array
    split_by : str or array-like, optional
        Splitting variable - column name or array
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional arguments (for compatibility)
        
    Returns
    -------
    MVPADesign
        The design object
    """
    # Parse y_train
    if y_train is None:
        raise ValueError("y_train must be provided")
    
    if isinstance(y_train, str) and train_design is not None:
        y_train = parse_variable(y_train, train_design)
    else:
        y_train = np.asarray(y_train)
    
    # Parse y_test
    if y_test is not None:
        if isinstance(y_test, str) and test_design is not None:
            y_test = parse_variable(y_test, test_design)
        else:
            y_test = np.asarray(y_test)
    
    # Parse block_var
    if block_var is not None:
        if isinstance(block_var, str) and train_design is not None:
            block_var = parse_variable(block_var, train_design)
        else:
            block_var = np.asarray(block_var)
    
    # Parse split_by
    if split_by is not None:
        if isinstance(split_by, str) and train_design is not None:
            split_by = parse_variable(split_by, train_design)
        else:
            split_by = np.asarray(split_by)
    
    return MVPADesign(
        y_train=y_train,
        y_test=y_test,
        train_design=train_design,
        test_design=test_design,
        block_var=block_var,
        split_by=split_by
    )

"""MVPA Design class for experimental design specification.

This module provides the MVPADesign class that encapsulates experimental
design information including response variables, blocking factors, and
splitting variables.
"""

from typing import Optional, Union, Dict, Any
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from dataclasses import dataclass


def parse_variable(var: Union[str, np.ndarray], 
                  design: Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]) -> np.ndarray:
    """Parse a variable specification from design matrix.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    var : str or array-like
        Variable specification - either column name or array
    design : DataFrame or dict
        Design matrix containing variables
        
    Returns
    -------
    np.ndarray
        The parsed variable
        
    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If variable not found in design
    """
    if isinstance(var, str):
        if isinstance(design, pd.DataFrame):
            if var not in design.columns:
                raise ValueError(f"Design does not contain variable named: {var}")
            return design[var].values
        else:
            if var not in design:
                raise ValueError(f"Design does not contain variable named: {var}")
            return design[var]
    elif isinstance(var, (np.ndarray, list)):
        return np.asarray(var)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Variable must be a string name or array-like")


@dataclass
class MVPADesign:
    """MVPA Design object for experimental design specification.
    
    This class encapsulates training and testing designs, response variables,
    and optional blocking and splitting factors. It is the Python equivalent
    of R's mvpa_design.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    y_train : array-like
        Training response variable (labels or continuous values)
    y_test : array-like, optional
        Test response variable
    train_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Training design matrix with additional covariates
    test_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Test design matrix
    block_var : array-like, optional
        Blocking variable for cross-validation
    split_by : array-like, optional
        Variable for splitting analyses
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    y_train : np.ndarray
        Training response variable
    y_test : np.ndarray or None
        Test response variable
    train_design : DataFrame or dict or None
        Training design matrix
    test_design : DataFrame or dict or None
        Test design matrix
    block_var : np.ndarray or None
        Blocking variable
    split_by : np.ndarray or None
        Splitting variable
    has_test_set : bool
        Whether design includes test set
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> # Basic design with only response variable
    >>> y = np.array(['A', 'B', 'A', 'B', 'A', 'B'])
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=y)
    
    >>> # Design with blocking variable
    >>> blocks = np.array([1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3])
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=y, block_var=blocks)
    
    >>> # Design with full design matrix
    >>> df = pd.DataFrame({
    ...     'condition': ['A', 'B', 'A', 'B'],
    ...     'subject': [1, 1, 2, 2]
    ... })
    >>> design = MVPADesign(y_train=df['condition'], train_design=df)
    """
    
    y_train: np.ndarray
    y_test: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    train_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None
    test_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None
    block_var: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    split_by: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        """Convert inputs to numpy arrays and validate."""
        # Ensure y_train is numpy array
        self.y_train = np.asarray(self.y_train)
        
        # Convert other variables if provided
        if self.y_test is not None:
            self.y_test = np.asarray(self.y_test)
            
        if self.block_var is not None:
            self.block_var = np.asarray(self.block_var)
            # Validate length
            if len(self.block_var) != len(self.y_train):
                raise ValueError(f"block_var length ({len(self.block_var)}) must match "
                               f"y_train length ({len(self.y_train)})")
        
        if self.split_by is not None:
            self.split_by = np.asarray(self.split_by)
            # Validate length
            if len(self.split_by) != len(self.y_train):
                raise ValueError(f"split_by length ({len(self.split_by)}) must match "
                               f"y_train length ({len(self.y_train)})")
    
    @property
    def has_test_set(self) -> bool:
        """Whether this design has a test set."""
        return self.y_test is not None
    
    @property
    def n_train(self) -> int:
        """Number of training observations."""
        return len(self.y_train)
    
    @property
    def n_test(self) -> int:
        """Number of test observations."""
        return 0 if self.y_test is None else len(self.y_test)
    
    @property
    def is_classification(self) -> bool:
        """Whether this is a classification problem."""
        # Check if y_train contains strings or has few unique values
        if self.y_train.dtype.kind in {'U', 'S', 'O'}:
            return True
        unique_vals = np.unique(self.y_train)
        # If numeric with few unique values, likely classification
        return len(unique_vals) < 0.1 * len(self.y_train)
    
    @property
    def n_classes(self) -> Optional[int]:
        """Number of classes for classification problems."""
        if self.is_classification:
            return len(np.unique(self.y_train))
        return None
    
    @property
    def classes(self) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
        """Unique class labels for classification problems."""
        if self.is_classification:
            return np.unique(self.y_train)
        return None
    
    def get_train_variable(self, name: str) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get variable from training design.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Variable name
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Variable values
        """
        if self.train_design is None:
            raise ValueError("No training design matrix available")
        return parse_variable(name, self.train_design)
    
    def get_test_variable(self, name: str) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get variable from test design.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        name : str
            Variable name
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Variable values
        """
        if self.test_design is None:
            raise ValueError("No test design matrix available")
        return parse_variable(name, self.test_design)
    
    def split_indices(self) -> Dict[Any, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices for each split group.
        
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Mapping from split values to indices
        """
        if self.split_by is None:
            raise ValueError("No split_by variable defined")
            
        indices = {}
        for val in np.unique(self.split_by):
            indices[val] = np.where(self.split_by == val)[0]
        return indices
    
    def block_indices(self) -> Dict[Any, np.ndarray]:
        """Get indices for each block.
        
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Mapping from block values to indices
        """
        if self.block_var is None:
            raise ValueError("No block_var defined")
            
        indices = {}
        for val in np.unique(self.block_var):
            indices[val] = np.where(self.block_var == val)[0]
        return indices
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        lines = ["MVPADesign"]
        lines.append(f"  Training samples : {self.n_train}")
        if self.has_test_set:
            lines.append(f"  Test samples     : {self.n_test}")
        
        if self.is_classification:
            lines.append(f"  Type             : Classification ({self.n_classes} classes)")
            lines.append(f"  Classes          : {self.classes}")
        else:
            lines.append(f"  Type             : Regression")
            
        if self.block_var is not None:
            n_blocks = len(np.unique(self.block_var))
            lines.append(f"  Blocks           : {n_blocks}")
            
        if self.split_by is not None:
            n_splits = len(np.unique(self.split_by))
            lines.append(f"  Split groups     : {n_splits}")
            
        return "\n".join(lines)


def mvpa_design(train_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None,
                test_design: Optional[Union[pd.DataFrame, Dict[str, np.ndarray]]] = None,
                y_train: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                y_test: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                block_var: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                split_by: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                **kwargs) -> MVPADesign:
    """Create an MVPA Design object.
    
    Convenience function that handles variable parsing from design matrices.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    train_design : DataFrame or dict, optional
        Training design matrix
    test_design : DataFrame or dict, optional  
        Test design matrix
    y_train : str or array-like
        Training response - column name or array
    y_test : str or array-like, optional
        Test response - column name or array
    block_var : str or array-like, optional
        Blocking variable - column name or array
    split_by : str or array-like, optional
        Splitting variable - column name or array
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional arguments (for compatibility)
        
    Returns
    -------
    MVPADesign
        The design object
    """
    # Parse y_train
    if y_train is None:
        raise ValueError("y_train must be provided")
    
    if isinstance(y_train, str) and train_design is not None:
        y_train = parse_variable(y_train, train_design)
    else:
        y_train = np.asarray(y_train)
    
    # Parse y_test
    if y_test is not None:
        if isinstance(y_test, str) and test_design is not None:
            y_test = parse_variable(y_test, test_design)
        else:
            y_test = np.asarray(y_test)
    
    # Parse block_var
    if block_var is not None:
        if isinstance(block_var, str) and train_design is not None:
            block_var = parse_variable(block_var, train_design)
        else:
            block_var = np.asarray(block_var)
    
    # Parse split_by
    if split_by is not None:
        if isinstance(split_by, str) and train_design is not None:
            split_by = parse_variable(split_by, train_design)
        else:
            split_by = np.asarray(split_by)
    
    return MVPADesign(
        y_train=y_train,
        y_test=y_test,
        train_design=train_design,
        test_design=test_design,
        block_var=block_var,
        split_by=split_by
    )

"""MVPA Model base class and model registry.

This module provides the base MVPAModel class and a registry system
for registering custom models.
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from typing import Dict, Type, Any, Optional, Union, Tuple
import numpy as np
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator

from .dataset import MVPADataset
from .design import MVPADesign


# Global model registry
_MODEL_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Type['MVPAModel']] = {}


def register_model(name: str):
    """Decorator to register a model class.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Name to register the model under
        
    Returns
    -------
    callable
        Decorator function
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> @register_model("my_classifier")
    ... class MyClassifier(MVPAModel):
    ...     pass
    """
    def decorator(cls: Type['MVPAModel']):
        _MODEL_REGISTRY[name] = cls
        return cls
    return decorator


def get_model(name: str) -> Type['MVPAModel']:
    """Get a registered model class by name.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Model name
        
    Returns
    -------
    Type[MVPAModel]
        The model class
        
    Raises
    ------
    KeyError
        If model not found
    """
    if name not in _MODEL_REGISTRY:
        raise KeyError(f"Model '{name}' not found. Available models: {list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())}")
    return _MODEL_REGISTRY[name]


def list_models() -> list:
    """List all registered models.
    
    Returns
    -------
    list
        List of registered model names
    """
    return list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())


class MVPAModel(ABC):
    """Abstract base class for MVPA models.
    
    This class defines the interface that all MVPA models must implement.
    It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_model.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset containing neuroimaging data
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model ('classification' or 'regression')
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional model-specific parameters
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model
    params : dict
        Model parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign, 
                 model_type: str = "classification", **kwargs):
        self.dataset = dataset
        self.design = design
        self.model_type = model_type
        self.params = kwargs
        
        # Validate model type
        if model_type not in ["classification", "regression"]:
            raise ValueError(f"model_type must be 'classification' or 'regression', got {model_type}")
        
        # Check compatibility
        if model_type == "classification" and not design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'classification' but design appears to be regression")
        elif model_type == "regression" and design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'regression' but design appears to be classification")
    
    @abstractmethod
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Fit the model to training data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Training data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray
            Training labels/values
        **fit_params : dict
            Additional fitting parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
            The fitted model
        """
        pass
    
    @abstractmethod
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions on new data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Test data (n_samples, n_features)
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Predictions
        """
        pass
    
    def fit_predict(self, X_train: np.ndarray, y_train: np.ndarray, 
                    X_test: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Fit model and make predictions.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X_train : np.ndarray
            Training data
        y_train : np.ndarray
            Training labels
        X_test : np.ndarray
            Test data
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Predictions
        """
        self.fit(X_train, y_train)
        return self.predict(X_test)
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        deep : bool
            Whether to return nested parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Model parameters
        """
        return self.params.copy()
    
    def set_params(self, **params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Set model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        **params : dict
            Parameters to set
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
        """
        self.params.update(params)
        return self
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"{self.__class__.__name__}\n"
                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
                f"  Parameters: {self.params}")


class SklearnModelWrapper(MVPAModel):
    """Wrapper for scikit-learn models.
    
    This class wraps any scikit-learn estimator to work with the MVPA framework.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    estimator : BaseEstimator
        Scikit-learn estimator instance
    model_type : str, optional
        Type of model (auto-detected if not provided)
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign,
                 estimator: BaseEstimator, model_type: Optional[str] = None, 
                 **kwargs):
        # Auto-detect model type if not provided
        if model_type is None:
            if hasattr(estimator, "predict_proba") or hasattr(estimator, "decision_function"):
                model_type = "classification"
            else:
                model_type = "regression"
        
        super().__init__(dataset, design, model_type, **kwargs)
        self.estimator = estimator
        self._is_fitted = False
    
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'SklearnModelWrapper':
        """Fit the sklearn model."""
        self.estimator.fit(X, y, **fit_params)
        self._is_fitted = True
        return self
    
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions using the sklearn model."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        return self.estimator.predict(X)
    
    def predict_proba(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Predict class probabilities (classification only)."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "predict_proba"):
            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support predict_proba")
        return self.estimator.predict_proba(X)
    
    def decision_function(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get decision function values (classification only)."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "decision_function"):
            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support decision_function")
        return self.estimator.decision_function(X)
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get parameters including sklearn estimator params."""
        params = super().get_params(deep)
        if hasattr(self.estimator, "get_params"):
            estimator_params = self.estimator.get_params(deep)
            params.update({f"estimator__{k}": v for k, v in estimator_params.items()})
        return params
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"SklearnModelWrapper\n"
                f"  Estimator: {self.estimator.__class__.__name__}\n"
                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
                f"  Fitted: {self._is_fitted}")

"""MVPA Model base class and model registry.

This module provides the base MVPAModel class and a registry system
for registering custom models.
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from typing import Dict, Type, Any, Optional, Union, Tuple
import numpy as np
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator

from .dataset import MVPADataset
from .design import MVPADesign


# Global model registry
_MODEL_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Type['MVPAModel']] = {}


def register_model(name: str):
    """Decorator to register a model class.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Name to register the model under
        
    Returns
    -------
    callable
        Decorator function
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> @register_model("my_classifier")
    ... class MyClassifier(MVPAModel):
    ...     pass
    """
    def decorator(cls: Type['MVPAModel']):
        _MODEL_REGISTRY[name] = cls
        return cls
    return decorator


def get_model(name: str) -> Type['MVPAModel']:
    """Get a registered model class by name.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Model name
        
    Returns
    -------
    Type[MVPAModel]
        The model class
        
    Raises
    ------
    KeyError
        If model not found
    """
    if name not in _MODEL_REGISTRY:
        raise KeyError(f"Model '{name}' not found. Available models: {list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())}")
    return _MODEL_REGISTRY[name]


def list_models() -> list:
    """List all registered models.
    
    Returns
    -------
    list
        List of registered model names
    """
    return list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())


class MVPAModel(ABC):
    """Abstract base class for MVPA models.
    
    This class defines the interface that all MVPA models must implement.
    It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_model.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset containing neuroimaging data
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model ('classification' or 'regression')
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional model-specific parameters
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model
    params : dict
        Model parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign, 
                 model_type: str = "classification", **kwargs):
        self.dataset = dataset
        self.design = design
        self.model_type = model_type
        self.params = kwargs
        
        # Validate model type
        if model_type not in ["classification", "regression"]:
            raise ValueError(f"model_type must be 'classification' or 'regression', got {model_type}")
        
        # Check compatibility
        if model_type == "classification" and not design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'classification' but design appears to be regression")
        elif model_type == "regression" and design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'regression' but design appears to be classification")
    
    @abstractmethod
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Fit the model to training data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Training data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray
            Training labels/values
        **fit_params : dict
            Additional fitting parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
            The fitted model
        """
        pass
    
    @abstractmethod
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions on new data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Test data (n_samples, n_features)
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Predictions
        """
        pass
    
    def fit_predict(self, X_train: np.ndarray, y_train: np.ndarray, 
                    X_test: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Fit model and make predictions.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X_train : np.ndarray
            Training data
        y_train : np.ndarray
            Training labels
        X_test : np.ndarray
            Test data
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Predictions
        """
        self.fit(X_train, y_train)
        return self.predict(X_test)
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        deep : bool
            Whether to return nested parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Model parameters
        """
        return self.params.copy()
    
    def set_params(self, **params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Set model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        **params : dict
            Parameters to set
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
        """
        self.params.update(params)
        return self
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"{self.__class__.__name__}\n"
                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
                f"  Parameters: {self.params}")


class SklearnModelWrapper(MVPAModel):
    """Wrapper for scikit-learn models.
    
    This class wraps any scikit-learn estimator to work with the MVPA framework.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    estimator : BaseEstimator
        Scikit-learn estimator instance
    model_type : str, optional
        Type of model (auto-detected if not provided)
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign,
                 estimator: BaseEstimator, model_type: Optional[str] = None, 
                 **kwargs):
        # Auto-detect model type if not provided
        if model_type is None:
            if hasattr(estimator, "predict_proba") or hasattr(estimator, "decision_function"):
                model_type = "classification"
            else:
                model_type = "regression"
        
        super().__init__(dataset, design, model_type, **kwargs)
        self.estimator = estimator
        self._is_fitted = False
    
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'SklearnModelWrapper':
        """Fit the sklearn model."""
        self.estimator.fit(X, y, **fit_params)
        self._is_fitted = True
        return self
    
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions using the sklearn model."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        return self.estimator.predict(X)
    
    def predict_proba(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Predict class probabilities (classification only)."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "predict_proba"):
            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support predict_proba")
        return self.estimator.predict_proba(X)
    
    def decision_function(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get decision function values (classification only)."""
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "decision_function"):
            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support decision_function")
        return self.estimator.decision_function(X)
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get parameters including sklearn estimator params."""
        params = super().get_params(deep)
        if hasattr(self.estimator, "get_params"):
            estimator_params = self.estimator.get_params(deep)
            params.update({f"estimator__{k}": v for k, v in estimator_params.items()})
        return params
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"SklearnModelWrapper\n"
                f"  Estimator: {self.estimator.__class__.__name__}\n"
                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
                f"  Fitted: {self._is_fitted}")

"""Tests for core MVPA classes."""

import numpy as np
import pytest
import sys
sys.path.append('/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/pyneuroim/src')
sys.path.append('/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/pyvoxpat')

from pyneuroim import DenseNeuroVec, DenseNeuroVol, NeuroSpace
from pyvoxpat.core import MVPADataset, MVPADesign, MVPAModel
from pyvoxpat.core.dataset import gen_sample_dataset


class TestMVPADataset:
    """Test MVPADataset class."""
    
    def test_create_dataset(self):
        """Test basic dataset creation."""
        # Create sample data
        space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 50])
        train_data = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space)
        
        mask_space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10])
        mask_data = np.ones((10, 10, 10))
        mask = DenseNeuroVol(mask_data, mask_space)
        
        # Create dataset
        dataset = MVPADataset(train_data, mask=mask)
        
        assert dataset.n_timepoints == 50
        assert dataset.n_voxels == 1000
        assert not dataset.has_test_set
    
    def test_dataset_with_test(self):
        """Test dataset with test data."""
        space_train = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 50])
        train_data = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 50), space_train)
        
        space_test = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10, 20])
        test_data = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(10, 10, 10, 20), space_test)
        
        mask_space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10])
        mask = DenseNeuroVol(np.ones((10, 10, 10)), mask_space)
        
        dataset = MVPADataset(train_data, test_data, mask=mask)
        
        assert dataset.has_test_set
        assert dataset.test_data.shape[3] == 20
    
    def test_invalid_mask(self):
        """Test error handling for invalid masks."""
        space = NeuroSpace(dim=[1, 1, 1, 50])
        train_data = DenseNeuroVec(np.random.randn(1, 1, 1, 50), space)
        
        mask_space = NeuroSpace(dim=[1, 1, 1])
        mask = DenseNeuroVol(np.ones((1, 1, 1)), mask_space)
        
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Only 1 voxel detected"):
            MVPADataset(train_data, mask=mask)
    
    def test_masked_data_extraction(self):
        """Test extracting masked data."""
        space = NeuroSpace(dim=[5, 5, 5, 10])
        data = np.random.randn(5, 5, 5, 10)
        train_data = DenseNeuroVec(data, space)
        
        # Create mask with specific pattern
        mask_space = NeuroSpace(dim=[5, 5, 5])
        mask_data = np.zeros((5, 5, 5))
        mask_data[1:4, 1:4, 1:4] = 1  # 3x3x3 = 27 voxels
        mask = DenseNeuroVol(mask_data, mask_space)
        
        dataset = MVPADataset(train_data, mask=mask)
        
        assert dataset.n_voxels == 27
        
        # Extract masked data
        masked = dataset.get_masked_data()
        assert masked.shape == (10, 27)  # timepoints x voxels


class TestMVPADesign:
    """Test MVPADesign class."""
    
    def test_basic_design(self):
        """Test basic design creation."""
        y = np.array(['A', 'B', 'A', 'B', 'A', 'B'])
        design = MVPADesign(y_train=y)
        
        assert design.n_train == 6
        assert design.is_classification
        assert design.n_classes == 2
        assert np.array_equal(design.classes, ['A', 'B'])
        assert not design.has_test_set
    
    def test_regression_design(self):
        """Test regression design."""
        y = np.random.randn(100)
        design = MVPADesign(y_train=y)
        
        assert design.n_train == 100
        assert not design.is_classification
        assert design.n_classes is None
    
    def test_design_with_blocks(self):
        """Test design with blocking variable."""
        y = np.repeat(['A', 'B'], 10)
        blocks = np.repeat(np.arange(5), 4)
        
        design = MVPADesign(y_train=y, block_var=blocks)
        
        block_idx = design.block_indices()
        assert len(block_idx) == 5
        assert all(len(idx) == 4 for idx in block_idx.values())
    
    def test_design_with_splits(self):
        """Test design with split variable."""
        y = np.array(['A', 'B'] * 10)
        splits = np.repeat(['G1', 'G2'], 10)
        
        design = MVPADesign(y_train=y, split_by=splits)
        
        split_idx = design.split_indices()
        assert len(split_idx) == 2
        assert len(split_idx['G1']) == 10
        assert len(split_idx['G2']) == 10


class TestGenSampleDataset:
    """Test sample dataset generation."""
    
    def test_generate_categorical(self):
        """Test generating categorical dataset."""
        result = gen_sample_dataset(
            D=(10, 10, 10),
            nobs=50,
            response_type="categorical",
            nlevels=2
        )
        
        assert 'dataset' in result
        assert 'design' in result
        
        dataset = result['dataset']
        design = result['design']
        
        assert dataset.n_timepoints == 50
        assert design.is_classification
        assert design.n_classes == 2
    
    def test_generate_continuous(self):
        """Test generating continuous dataset."""
        result = gen_sample_dataset(
            D=(8, 8, 8),
            nobs=100,
            response_type="continuous"
        )
        
        design = result['design']
        assert not design.is_classification
        assert design.n_train == 100
    
    def test_generate_with_test(self):
        """Test generating dataset with test set."""
        result = gen_sample_dataset(
            D=(5, 5, 5),
            nobs=40,
            response_type="categorical",
            external_test=True,
            ntest_obs=20
        )
        
        dataset = result['dataset']
        design = result['design']
        
        assert dataset.has_test_set
        assert design.has_test_set
        assert design.n_test == 20


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pytest.main([__file__, "-v"])

content.text:
## Summary

I've successfully started the conversion of the rMVPA R package to Python (pyvoxpat). Here's what has been completed:

### ✅ Completed:
1. **Project Structure** - Created proper Python package structure with pyproject.toml
2. **Core Data Structures**:
   - `MVPADataset` - For volumetric neuroimaging data (using pyneuroim)
   - `MVPADesign` - For experimental design specification
   - `MVPAModel` - Base class and model registry system
   - `gen_sample_dataset` - Function to generate synthetic datasets
3. **Basic Testing** - Created and ran 11 passing tests for the core functionality

### 🔧 Architecture Decisions:
- Used your existing `pyneuroim` package for neuroimaging data structures
- Deferred `MVPASurfaceDataset` until neurosurf is ported
- Created placeholder modules for cross-validation, analysis, and metrics
- Followed Python best practices with type hints and dataclasses
- Maintained API similarity to R version while being Pythonic

### 📋 Next Priority Tasks:
1. Implement cross-validation schemes (blocked, k-fold, bootstrap)
2. Port custom classifiers (corclass, SDA variants, LDA variants)
3. Implement searchlight analysis using pyneuroim
4. Create comprehensive test suite

The foundation is now in place to continue porting the remaining functionality. Would you like me to proceed with implementing the cross-validation schemes next?

content.text: Now let me look at the specific cross-validation functions:

bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation    package:rMVPA    R Documentation

_ b_ o_ o_ t_ s_ t_ r_ a_ p__ b_ l_ o_ c_ k_ e_ d__ c_ r_ o_ s_ s__ v_ a_ l_ i_ d_ a_ t_ i_ o_ n

_ D_ e_ s_ c_ r_ i_ p_ t_ i_ o_ n:

     Bootstrap Blocked Cross-Validation Specification

     This function constructs a cross-validation specification using a
     predefined blocking variable.

     This function constructs a cross-validation specification using a
     predefined blocking variable, dividing each block into a specified
     number of folds.

     This function constructs a cross-validation specification that
     uses a user-supplied set of training and test indices.

_ U_ s_ a_ g_ e:

     bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, nreps = 10, weights = NULL)
     
     blocked_cross_validation(block_var)
     
     sequential_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, nfolds = 2, nreps = 4)
     
     custom_cross_validation(sample_set)
     
_ A_ r_ g_ u_ m_ e_ n_ t_ s:

block_var: An integer vector indicating the cross-validation blocks.
          Each block is indicated by a unique integer.

   nreps: The number of repetitions for the cross-validation procedure.

 weights: A numeric vector of the same length as `block_var`,
          representing the weights for each sample. Higher weights
          indicate that observations will be sampled more often. If not
          provided, all samples are treated as equally likely.

  nfolds: The number of folds to divide each sequence of trials within
          a block.

sample_set: A list of training and test sample indices. Each element of
          the list must be a named list with two elements: "train" and
          "test".

_ D_ e_ t_ a_ i_ l_ s:

     This function constructs a cross-validation specification using a
     predefined blocking variable and creates bootstrap resamples
     within the blocks.

     The function first checks if the provided weights are non-negative
     and normalizes them to sum to 1. It then constructs a list
     containing the block variable, number of folds, block indices,
     number of repetitions, and weights. The output list is assigned
     the class `"bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation"`,
     `"cross_validation"`, and `"list"`.

     The function constructs a list containing the block variable,
     number of folds, and block indices. The output list is assigned
     the class `"blocked_cross_validation"`, `"cross_validation"`, and
     `"list"`.

     The function constructs a list containing the block variable,
     number of folds, number of repetitions, and block indices. The
     output list is assigned the class
     `"sequential_blocked_cross_validation"`, `"cross_validation"`, and
     `"list"`.

     The custom_cross_validation class allows users to define their own
     cross-validation structure by providing a set of training and test
     indices. This can be useful in situations where the standard
     cross-validation methods (e.g., k-fold, leave-one-out) do not
     adequately represent the desired validation structure.

     The function constructs a list containing the sample set and the
     number of folds, derived from the length of the sample set. The
     output list is assigned the class `"custom_cross_validation"`,
     `"cross_validation"`, and `"list"`.

_ V_ a_ l_ u_ e:

     A list containing the cross-validation specification, with class
     attributes "bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation",
     "cross_validation", and "list".

     A list containing the cross-validation specification, with class
     attributes "blocked_cross_validation", "cross_validation", and
     "list".

     A list containing the cross-validation specification, with class
     attributes "sequential_blocked_cross_validation",
     "cross_validation", and "list".

     A list containing the custom cross-validation specification, with
     class attributes "custom_cross_validation", "cross_validation",
     and "list".

_ E_ x_ a_ m_ p_ l_ e_ s:

     block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50)
     weights <- runif(length(block_var))
     weights[1] = 0
     cval <- bootstrap_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, weights=weights)
     X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10)
     y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))
     
     sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y)
     block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50)
     cval <- blocked_cross_validation(block_var)
     X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10)
     y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))
     
     sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y)
     block_var <- rep(1:5, each=50)
     nfolds <- 2
     nreps <- 4
     cval <- sequential_blocked_cross_validation(block_var, nfolds, nreps)
     X <- matrix(rnorm(length(block_var) * 10), length(block_var), 10)
     y <- rep(letters[1:5], length.out=length(block_var))
     
     sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y)
     sample_set <- list(
       list(train = 1:80, test = 81:100),
       list(train = 1:60, test = 61:100),
       list(train = 1:40, test = 41:100)
     )
     cval <- custom_cross_validation(sample_set)
     X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 10), 100, 10)
     y <- rep(letters[1:4], length.out=100)
     
     sam <- crossval_samples(cval, as.data.frame(X), y)

          provided, all samples are treated as equally likely.

          a block.

content.text: Now let me check the source code of the cross-validation functions to understand their implementation:

content.content:
1→#' @noRd
     2→#' @keywords internal
     3→check_len <- function(y, block_var) {
     4→  futile.logger::flog.debug("Checking length of y and block_var")
     5→  futile.logger::flog.debug("y: %s", paste(dim(y), collapse=" x "))
     6→  futile.logger::flog.debug("block_var: %s", length(block_var))
     7→  if (is.vector(y)) {
     8→    if (!length(block_var) == length(y)) {
     9→      stop("length of `block_var` must be equal to length(y)", call. = FALSE)
    10→    }
    11→  } else if (is.matrix(y)) {
    12→    if (!nrow(y) == length(block_var)) {
    13→      stop("number of rows in `y` must be equal to length(block_var)", call. = FALSE)
    14→    }
    15→  }
    16→}
    17→
    18→
    19→#' @noRd
    20→#' @keywords internal
    21→subset_y <- function(y, idx) {
    22→  if (is.vector(y) || is.factor(y)) {
    23→    y[idx]
    24→  } else if (is.matrix(y)) {
    25→    y[idx,,drop=FALSE]
    26→  }
    27→}
    28→
    29→#' K-fold Cross-Validation Data Preparation
    30→#'
    31→#' This function prepares the data for k-fold cross-validation by dividing the
    32→#' dataset into k folds. It creates subsets of training and testing data for
    33→#' each fold without performing any analysis or fitting models.
    34→#'
    35→#' @param data A data frame containing the training data.
    36→#' @param y A response vector.
    37→#' @param k An integer specifying the number of folds for cross-validation.
    38→#' @param id A character string specifying the identifier for the output data frame.
    39→#' @return A tibble containing the training and testing data, response vectors, and fold IDs for each fold.
    40→#' @examples
    41→#' data <- iris[,-5]
    42→#' y <- iris$Species
    43→#' result <- crossv_k(data, y, k = 5)
    44→#' @importFrom modelr resample
    45→#' @export
    46→crossv_k <- function(data, y, k = 5, id = ".id") {
    47→  if (!is.numeric(k) || length(k) != 1) {
    48→    stop("`k` must be a single integer.", call. = FALSE)
    49→  }
    50→  
    51→  if (k < 2) {
    52→    stop("`k` must be at least 2 for cross-validation.", call. = FALSE)
    53→  }
    54→  
    55→  n <- nrow(data)
    56→  folds <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = n))
    57→  check_len(y, folds) # Ensure y and the generated folds are compatible in length
    58→  
    59→  idx <- seq_len(n)
    60→  fold_idx <- split(idx, folds)
    61→  
    62→  fold <- function(test) {
    63→    tidx <- setdiff(idx, test)
    64→    list(
    65→      ytrain = subset_y(y, tidx),
    66→      ytest = subset_y(y, test),
    67→      train = modelr::resample(data, setdiff(idx, test)),
    68→      test = modelr::resample(data, test)
    69→    )
    70→  }
    71→  
    72→  
    73→  cols <- purrr::transpose(purrr::map(fold_idx, fold))
    74→  cols[[id]] <- gen_id(k)
    75→  
    76→  tibble::as_tibble(cols, .name_repair = "unique")
    77→}
    78→
    79→#' Repeated Two-Fold Cross-Validation Data Preparation
    80→#'
    81→#' This function prepares the data for repeated two-fold cross-validation by
    82→#' dividing the dataset into two folds based on the provided block variable.
    83→#' It creates subsets of training and testing data for each repetition without
    84→#' performing any analysis or fitting models.
    85→#'
    86→#' @param data A data frame containing the training data.
    87→#' @param y A response vector.
    88→#' @param block_var An integer vector defining the cross-validation blocks.
    89→#' @param block_ind A vector containing the ordered integer IDs of the blocks (optional).
    90→#' @param id A character string specifying the identifier for the output data frame.
    91→#' @param nreps An integer specifying the number of repetitions for two-fold cross-validation.
    92→#' @return A tibble containing the training and testing data, response vectors, and fold IDs for each repetition.
    93→#' @examples
    94→#' X <- data.frame(x1 = rnorm(100), x2 = rnorm(100))
    95→#' y <- rep(letters[1:4], 25)
    96→#' block_var <- rep(1:4, each = 25)
    97→#' cv <- crossv_twofold(X, y, block_var, nreps = 10)
    98→#' @noRd
    99→crossv_twofold <- function(data, y, block_var, block_ind=NULL, id = ".id", nreps=15) {
   100→  ## every time this is called, it regenerates new indices
   101→  if (nreps < 2) {
   102→    stop("'nreps' must be at least 2")
   103→  }
   104→  
   105→  check_len(y, block_var)
   106→  
   107→  if (is.null(block_ind)) {
