"""RSA design class and utilities."""

import numpy as np
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Union, Any
from scipy.spatial.distance import squareform
import pandas as pd
import warnings


def sanitize(name: str) -> str:
    """Sanitize variable names for use in formulas.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        The name to sanitize
        
    Returns
    -------
    str
        Sanitized name
    """
    name = name.replace(":", ".")
    name = name.replace(" ", "")
    name = name.replace("(", ".")
    name = name.replace(")", ".")
    name = name.replace(",", "_")
    if name.endswith("."):
        name = name[:-1]
    return name


class RSADesign:
    """Design for RSA (Representational Similarity Analysis) model.
    
    This class constructs a design for an RSA model using dissimilarity matrices
    and auxiliary variables.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    formula : str
        Formula expression specifying the dissimilarity-based regression function
    data : dict
        Named dictionary containing dissimilarity matrices and auxiliary variables
    block_var : str, array-like, optional
        Block structure variable name or array
    split_by : str, optional
        Variable name for grouping structure when evaluating test performance
    keep_intra_run : bool, default=False
        Whether to include within-run comparisons
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    formula : str
        The input formula
    data : dict
        The input data
    split_by : str
        The split_by variable
    split_groups : dict
        Grouping structure for split_by
    block_var : array-like
        Block structure
    include : array-like
        Logical vector for including/excluding comparisons
    model_mat : np.ndarray
        Model matrix for RSA analysis
    """
    
    def __init__(self, formula: str, data: Dict[str, Any], 
                 block_var: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
                 split_by: Optional[str] = None, 
                 keep_intra_run: bool = False):
        
        self.formula = formula
        self.data = data
        self.split_by = split_by
        self.keep_intra_run = keep_intra_run
        
        # Validate data - all elements must have same size
        sizes = []
        for key, val in data.items():
            if isinstance(val, np.ndarray):
                if val.ndim == 2:
                    assert val.shape[0] == val.shape[1], f"Matrix {key} must be square"
                    sizes.append(val.shape[0])
                elif val.ndim == 1:
                    # Check if it's a condensed distance matrix
                    n = int((1 + np.sqrt(1 + 8 * len(val))) / 2)
                    if n * (n - 1) // 2 == len(val):
                        sizes.append(n)
                    else:
                        sizes.append(len(val))
            elif hasattr(val, '__len__'):
                sizes.append(len(val))
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Illegal variable type for {key}: {type(val)}")
        
        if not all(s == sizes[0] for s in sizes):
            raise ValueError("All elements in 'data' must have the same number of rows/observations")
        
        self.n_obs = sizes[0]
        
        # Process split_by
        self.split_groups = None
        if split_by is not None:
            if split_by not in data:
                raise ValueError(f"split_by variable '{split_by}' not found in data")
            split_var = data[split_by]
            
            # Check minimum group sizes
            unique_vals, counts = np.unique(split_var, return_counts=True)
            if np.min(counts) < 3:
                raise ValueError("Splitting condition results in fewer than 3 observations in at least one set")
            
            # Create split groups
            self.split_groups = {}
            for val in unique_vals:
                self.split_groups[val] = np.where(split_var == val)[0]
        
        # Process block_var
        self.block_var = None
        if block_var is not None:
            if isinstance(block_var, str):
                if block_var not in data:
                    raise ValueError(f"block_var '{block_var}' not found in data")
                self.block_var = data[block_var]
            else:
                self.block_var = np.asarray(block_var)
                if len(self.block_var) != self.n_obs:
                    raise ValueError("block_var length must match number of observations")
        
        # Create include mask for within-run comparisons
        self.include = None
        if self.block_var is not None and not keep_intra_run:
            # Create pairwise comparison of block variables
            block_dist = np.zeros((self.n_obs, self.n_obs))
            for i in range(self.n_obs):
                for j in range(self.n_obs):
                    block_dist[i, j] = 0 if self.block_var[i] == self.block_var[j] else 1
            
            # Convert to condensed form
            self.include = squareform(block_dist, checks=False) != 0
        
        # Create model matrix
        self.model_mat = self._create_model_matrix()
    
    def _create_model_matrix(self) -> np.ndarray:
        """Create the model matrix from the formula and data.
        
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Model matrix
        """
        # Parse formula
        formula_str = self.formula.strip()
        if formula_str.startswith("~"):
            formula_str = formula_str[1:].strip()
        
        # Split by + to get individual terms
        terms = [t.strip() for t in formula_str.split("+")]
        
        # Collect matrices for each term
        matrices = []
        for term in terms:
            if term in self.data:
                mat = self.data[term]
                
                # Convert to condensed form if needed
                if isinstance(mat, np.ndarray) and mat.ndim == 2:
                    mat = squareform(mat, checks=False)
                elif hasattr(mat, 'condensed_form'):
                    mat = mat.condensed_form()
                
                # Ensure it's a 1D array
                mat = np.asarray(mat).flatten()
                
                # Apply include mask if present
                if self.include is not None:
                    mat = mat[self.include]
                
                matrices.append(mat)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Variable '{term}' not found in data")
        
        # Stack matrices as columns
        if matrices:
            model_mat = np.column_stack(matrices)
        else:
            raise ValueError("No valid terms found in formula")
        
        return model_mat
    
    def get_response_distances(self, response_data: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get response distances in appropriate format.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        response_data : np.ndarray
            Response data matrix (n_observations x n_features)
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Condensed distance vector
        """
        from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist
        
        # Compute pairwise distances
        distances = pdist(response_data, metric='correlation')
        
        # Apply include mask if present
        if self.include is not None:
            distances = distances[self.include]
        
        return distances


def rsa_design(formula: str, data: Dict[str, Any], 
               block_var: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
               split_by: Optional[str] = None, 
               keep_intra_run: bool = False) -> RSADesign:
    """Construct a design for an RSA model.
    
    This function constructs a design for an RSA model using the provided formula,
    data, and optional parameters.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    formula : str
        Formula expression specifying the dissimilarity-based regression function
    data : dict
        Named dictionary containing dissimilarity matrices and auxiliary variables
    block_var : str, array-like, optional
        Block structure variable name or array
    split_by : str, optional
        Variable name for grouping structure when evaluating test performance
    keep_intra_run : bool, default=False
        Whether to include within-run comparisons
        
    Returns
    -------
    RSADesign
        The RSA design object
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform
    >>> # Create some example data
    >>> X = np.random.randn(100, 20)
    >>> dismat = squareform(pdist(X, metric='euclidean'))
    >>> rdes = rsa_design("~ dismat", {'dismat': dismat})
    """
    return RSADesign(formula, data, block_var, split_by, keep_intra_run)

        if split_by is not None:
            if split_by not in data:
                raise ValueError(f"split_by variable '{split_by}' not found in data")
            split_var = data[split_by]
            
            # Check minimum group sizes
            unique_vals, counts = np.unique(split_var, return_counts=True)
            if np.min(counts) < 3:
                raise ValueError("Splitting condition results in fewer than 3 observations in at least one set")
            
            # Create split groups
            self.split_groups = {}
            for val in unique_vals:
                self.split_groups[val] = np.where(split_var == val)[0]
        
        # Process block_var
        self.block_var = None
        if block_var is not None:
            if isinstance(block_var, str):
                if block_var not in data:
                    raise ValueError(f"block_var '{block_var}' not found in data")
                self.block_var = data[block_var]
            else:
                self.block_var = np.asarray(block_var)
                if len(self.block_var) != self.n_obs:
                    raise ValueError("block_var length must match number of observations")
        
        # Create include mask for within-run comparisons
        self.include = None
        if self.block_var is not None and not keep_intra_run:
            # Create pairwise comparison of block variables
            block_dist = np.zeros((self.n_obs, self.n_obs))
            for i in range(self.n_obs):
                for j in range(self.n_obs):
                    block_dist[i, j] = 0 if self.block_var[i] == self.block_var[j] else 1
            
            # Convert to condensed form
            self.include = squareform(block_dist, checks=False) != 0
        
        # Create model matrix
        self.model_mat = self._create_model_matrix()
    
    def _create_model_matrix(self) -> np.ndarray:
        """Create the model matrix from the formula and data.
        
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Model matrix
        """
        # Parse formula
        formula_str = self.formula.strip()
        if formula_str.startswith("~"):
            formula_str = formula_str[1:].strip()
        
        # Split by + to get individual terms
        terms = [t.strip() for t in formula_str.split("+")]
        
        # Collect matrices for each term
        matrices = []
        for term in terms:
            if term in self.data:
                mat = self.data[term]
                
                # Convert to condensed form if needed
                if isinstance(mat, np.ndarray) and mat.ndim == 2:
                    mat = squareform(mat, checks=False)
                elif hasattr(mat, 'condensed_form'):
                    mat = mat.condensed_form()
                
                # Ensure it's a 1D array
                mat = np.asarray(mat).flatten()
                
                # Apply include mask if present
                if self.include is not None:
                    mat = mat[self.include]
                
                matrices.append(mat)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Variable '{term}' not found in data")
        
        # Stack matrices as columns
        if matrices:
            model_mat = np.column_stack(matrices)
        else:
            raise ValueError("No valid terms found in formula")
        
        return model_mat
    
    def get_response_distances(self, response_data: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Get response distances in appropriate format.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        response_data : np.ndarray
            Response data matrix (n_observations x n_features)
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Condensed distance vector
        """
        from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist
        
        # Compute pairwise distances
        distances = pdist(response_data, metric='correlation')
        
        # Apply include mask if present
        if self.include is not None:
            distances = distances[self.include]
        
        return distances


def rsa_design(formula: str, data: Dict[str, Any], 
               block_var: Optional[Union[str, np.ndarray]] = None,
               split_by: Optional[str] = None, 
               keep_intra_run: bool = False) -> RSADesign:
    """Construct a design for an RSA model.
    
    This function constructs a design for an RSA model using the provided formula,
    data, and optional parameters.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    formula : str
        Formula expression specifying the dissimilarity-based regression function
    data : dict
        Named dictionary containing dissimilarity matrices and auxiliary variables
    block_var : str, array-like, optional
        Block structure variable name or array
    split_by : str, optional
        Variable name for grouping structure when evaluating test performance
    keep_intra_run : bool, default=False
        Whether to include within-run comparisons
        
    Returns
    -------
    RSADesign
        The RSA design object
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform
    >>> # Create some example data
    >>> X = np.random.randn(100, 20)
    >>> dismat = squareform(pdist(X, metric='euclidean'))
    >>> rdes = rsa_design("~ dismat", {'dismat': dismat})
    """
    return RSADesign(formula, data, block_var, split_by, keep_intra_run)

input.content:
"""RSA model implementation."""

import numpy as np
from typing import Optional, Union, Dict, Any, Tuple
from scipy import stats
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform

from ...core.model import MVPAModel, register_model
from ...core.dataset import MVPADataset
from ...core.design import MVPADesign
from .rsa_design import RSADesign, rsa_design


class RSAModel(MVPAModel):
    """Representational Similarity Analysis (RSA) model.
    
    This model performs RSA by relating brain dissimilarity patterns to
    model dissimilarity patterns using regression.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign or RSADesign
        The experimental design or RSA design
    formula : str, optional
        Formula for RSA regression if design is MVPADesign
    model_data : dict, optional
        Model dissimilarity matrices if design is MVPADesign
    metric : str, default='correlation'
        Distance metric for computing brain dissimilarities
    method : str, default='pearson'
        Correlation method ('pearson', 'spearman', 'kendall')
    **kwargs
        Additional parameters
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    rsa_design_ : RSADesign
        The RSA design object
    model_ : LinearRegression
        Fitted regression model
    r_squared_ : float
        R-squared value of the fit
    correlation_ : float
        Correlation between predicted and actual dissimilarities
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: Union[MVPADesign, RSADesign],
                 formula: Optional[str] = None, model_data: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
                 metric: str = 'correlation', method: str = 'pearson', **kwargs):
        
        super().__init__(dataset, design, model_type="rsa", **kwargs)
        
        self.metric = metric
        self.method = method
        
        # Create RSA design if needed
        if isinstance(design, RSADesign):
            self.rsa_design_ = design
        else:
            # Need formula and model_data to create RSA design
            if formula is None or model_data is None:
                raise ValueError("formula and model_data required when design is MVPADesign")
            
            # Add block_var if present in MVPADesign
            block_var = None
            if hasattr(design, 'block_var') and design.block_var is not None:
                model_data = model_data.copy()
                model_data['block_var'] = design.block_var
                block_var = 'block_var'
            
            self.rsa_design_ = rsa_design(formula, model_data, block_var=block_var)
        
        self.model_ = None
        self.r_squared_ = None
        self.correlation_ = None
        self._is_fitted = False
    
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: Optional[np.ndarray] = None, **fit_params) -> 'RSAModel':
        """Fit the RSA model.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Brain data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray, optional
            Not used, included for API consistency
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
        """
        # Compute brain dissimilarities
        brain_distances = pdist(X, metric=self.metric)
        
        # Apply include mask if present
        if self.rsa_design_.include is not None:
            brain_distances = brain_distances[self.rsa_design_.include]
        
        # Get model matrix
        model_mat = self.rsa_design_.model_mat
        
        # Fit regression model
        self.model_ = LinearRegression()
        self.model_.fit(model_mat, brain_distances)
        
        # Compute fit statistics
        predicted = self.model_.predict(model_mat)
        self.r_squared_ = r2_score(brain_distances, predicted)
        
        # Compute correlation
        if self.method == 'pearson':
            self.correlation_, _ = stats.pearsonr(brain_distances, predicted)
        elif self.method == 'spearman':
            self.correlation_, _ = stats.spearmanr(brain_distances, predicted)
        elif self.method == 'kendall':
            self.correlation_, _ = stats.kendalltau(brain_distances, predicted)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown correlation method: {self.method}")
        
        self._is_fitted = True
        return self
    
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Predict brain dissimilarities from model dissimilarities.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Model dissimilarity matrix or matrices
            
        Returns
        -------
        np.ndarray
            Predicted brain dissimilarities
        """
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
        
        return self.model_.predict(X)
    
    def score(self, X: np.ndarray, y: Optional[np.ndarray] = None) -> float:
        """Compute RSA score (correlation) for test data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Test brain data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray, optional
            Not used
            
        Returns
        -------
        float
            Correlation between predicted and actual dissimilarities
        """
        if not self._is_fitted:
            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before scoring")
        
        # Compute brain dissimilarities
        brain_distances = pdist(X, metric=self.metric)
        
        # Apply include mask if present
        if self.rsa_design_.include is not None:
            brain_distances = brain_distances[self.rsa_design_.include]
        
        # Get predictions
        predicted = self.model_.predict(self.rsa_design_.model_mat)
        
        # Compute correlation
        if self.method == 'pearson':
            corr, _ = stats.pearsonr(brain_distances, predicted)
        elif self.method == 'spearman':
            corr, _ = stats.spearmanr(brain_distances, predicted)
        elif self.method == 'kendall':
            corr, _ = stats.kendalltau(brain_distances, predicted)
        
        return corr
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get parameters."""
        params = super().get_params(deep)
        params.update({
            'metric': self.metric,
            'method': self.method
        })
        return params


# Register the model
@register_model("rsa")
class RegisteredRSAModel(RSAModel):
    """Registered version of RSAModel."""
    pass


# Factory function
def rsa_model(dataset: MVPADataset, design: Union[MVPADesign, RSADesign],
              formula: Optional[str] = None, model_data: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
              metric: str = 'correlation', method: str = 'pearson', **kwargs) -> RSAModel:
    """Create an RSA model.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign or RSADesign
        The experimental design or RSA design
    formula : str, optional
        Formula for RSA regression if design is MVPADesign
    model_data : dict, optional
        Model dissimilarity matrices if design is MVPADesign
    metric : str, default='correlation'
        Distance metric for computing brain dissimilarities
    method : str, default='pearson'
        Correlation method ('pearson', 'spearman', 'kendall')
    **kwargs
        Additional parameters
        
    Returns
    -------
    RSAModel
        The RSA model instance
    """
    return RSAModel(dataset, design, formula, model_data, metric, method, **kwargs)

"""Tests for RSA models."""

import numpy as np
import pytest
import sys
sys.path.append('/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/pyneuroim/src')
sys.path.append('/Users/bbuchsbaum/code/pyvoxpat')

from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform
from pyvoxpat.models import RSADesign, rsa_design, RSAModel, rsa_model
from pyvoxpat.core import MVPADataset, MVPADesign, get_model
from pyvoxpat.core.dataset import gen_sample_dataset


class TestRSADesign:
    """Test RSA design creation."""
    
    def test_basic_design(self):
        """Test basic RSA design creation."""
        # Create example data
        n_obs = 50
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 20)
        
        # Create dissimilarity matrices
        dismat1 = squareform(pdist(X, metric='euclidean'))
        dismat2 = squareform(pdist(X * 2, metric='correlation'))
        
        # Create design
        data = {'dismat1': dismat1, 'dismat2': dismat2}
        design = rsa_design("~ dismat1 + dismat2", data)
        
        assert isinstance(design, RSADesign)
        assert design.n_obs == n_obs
        assert design.model_mat.shape[1] == 2  # Two predictors
        
    def test_condensed_form(self):
        """Test with condensed distance matrices."""
        n_obs = 30
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 15)
        
        # Use condensed form directly
        dismat1 = pdist(X, metric='euclidean')
        dismat2 = pdist(X, metric='correlation')
        
        data = {'dist1': dismat1, 'dist2': dismat2}
        design = rsa_design("~ dist1 + dist2", data)
        
        # Check model matrix has correct shape
        n_pairs = n_obs * (n_obs - 1) // 2
        assert design.model_mat.shape == (n_pairs, 2)
    
    def test_block_variable(self):
        """Test block variable handling."""
        n_obs = 40
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 10)
        
        # Create block structure (4 runs of 10 observations each)
        blocks = np.repeat([1, 2, 3, 4], 10)
        
        dismat = squareform(pdist(X))
        data = {'dismat': dismat, 'run': blocks}
        
        # Without keeping intra-run comparisons
        design1 = rsa_design("~ dismat", data, block_var='run', keep_intra_run=False)
        
        # With keeping intra-run comparisons
        design2 = rsa_design("~ dismat", data, block_var='run', keep_intra_run=True)
        
        # First design should have fewer comparisons
        assert design1.model_mat.shape[0] < design2.model_mat.shape[0]
        assert design1.include is not None
        assert design2.include is None
    
    def test_split_by(self):
        """Test split_by functionality."""
        n_obs = 60
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 15)
        
        # Create groups for splitting
        groups = np.repeat(['A', 'B', 'C'], 20)
        
        dismat = squareform(pdist(X))
        data = {'dismat': dismat, 'group': groups}
        
        design = rsa_design("~ dismat", data, split_by='group')
        
        assert design.split_groups is not None
        assert len(design.split_groups) == 3
        assert all(len(indices) == 20 for indices in design.split_groups.values())


class TestRSAModel:
    """Test RSA model functionality."""
    
    def test_basic_rsa(self):
        """Test basic RSA analysis."""
        # Create synthetic data with known structure
        n_obs = 50
        n_features = 100
        
        # Create model RDM (e.g., based on some categories)
        categories = np.repeat([0, 1, 2, 3, 4], 10)
        model_rdm = squareform(pdist(categories.reshape(-1, 1)))
        
        # Create brain data that partially follows the model
        brain_data = np.random.randn(n_obs, n_features)
        # Add category structure to brain data
        for i in range(5):
            brain_data[categories == i] += np.random.randn(n_features) * 2
        
        # Create dataset and design
        result = gen_sample_dataset(D=(10, 10, 1), nobs=n_obs)
        dataset = result['dataset']
        
        # Create RSA design
        data = {'model_rdm': model_rdm}
        rsa_des = rsa_design("~ model_rdm", data)
        
        # Create and fit model
        model = rsa_model(dataset, rsa_des)
        model.fit(brain_data)
        
        # Check that model is fitted
        assert model._is_fitted
        assert model.correlation_ is not None
        assert model.r_squared_ is not None
        
        # Correlation should be positive (brain follows model structure)
        assert model.correlation_ > 0
    
    def test_multiple_predictors(self):
        """Test RSA with multiple model RDMs."""
        n_obs = 40
        n_features = 50
        
        # Create two model RDMs
        # Model 1: Category structure
        cat1 = np.repeat([0, 1], 20)
        model_rdm1 = squareform(pdist(cat1.reshape(-1, 1)))
        
        # Model 2: Different category structure
        cat2 = np.tile([0, 1], 20)
        model_rdm2 = squareform(pdist(cat2.reshape(-1, 1)))
        
        # Create brain data influenced by both models
        brain_data = np.random.randn(n_obs, n_features)
        brain_data[cat1 == 0] += 1
        brain_data[cat2 == 0] += 0.5
        
        # Setup
        result = gen_sample_dataset(D=(5, 5, 2), nobs=n_obs)
        dataset = result['dataset']
        
        data = {'model1': model_rdm1, 'model2': model_rdm2}

        model = RSAModel(dataset, rsa_des)
        model.fit(brain_data)
        
        # Check model has correct number of coefficients
        assert len(model.model_.coef_) == 2
    
    def test_correlation_methods(self):
        """Test different correlation methods."""
        n_obs = 30
        n_features = 40
        
        # Create data
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 10)
        model_rdm = squareform(pdist(X))
        brain_data = np.random.randn(n_obs, n_features)
        
        result = gen_sample_dataset(D=(4, 4, 2), nobs=n_obs)
        dataset = result['dataset']
        
        data = {'model_rdm': model_rdm}
        rsa_des = rsa_design("~ model_rdm", data)

    def test_distance_metrics(self):

class TestRSADesign:
    """Test RSA design creation."""
    
    def test_basic_design(self):
        """Test basic RSA design creation."""
        # Create example data
        n_obs = 50
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 20)
        
        # Create dissimilarity matrices
        dismat1 = squareform(pdist(X, metric='euclidean'))
        dismat2 = squareform(pdist(X * 2, metric='correlation'))
        
        # Create design
        data = {'dismat1': dismat1, 'dismat2': dismat2}
        design = rsa_design("~ dismat1 + dismat2", data)
        
        assert isinstance(design, RSADesign)
        assert design.n_obs == n_obs
        assert design.model_mat.shape[1] == 2  # Two predictors
        
    def test_condensed_form(self):
        """Test with condensed distance matrices."""
        n_obs = 30
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 15)
        
        # Use condensed form directly
        dismat1 = pdist(X, metric='euclidean')
        dismat2 = pdist(X, metric='correlation')
        
        data = {'dist1': dismat1, 'dist2': dismat2}
        design = rsa_design("~ dist1 + dist2", data)
        
        # Check model matrix has correct shape
        n_pairs = n_obs * (n_obs - 1) // 2
        assert design.model_mat.shape == (n_pairs, 2)
    
    def test_block_variable(self):
        """Test block variable handling."""
        n_obs = 40
        X = np.random.randn(n_obs, 10)
        
        # Create block structure (4 runs of 10 observations each)
        blocks = np.repeat([1, 2, 3, 4], 10)
        
        dismat = squareform(pdist(X))
        data = {'dismat': dismat, 'run': blocks}
        
        # Without keeping intra-run comparisons

        # First design should have fewer comparisons
        assert design1.model_mat.shape[0] < design2.model_mat.shape[0]
        assert design1.include is not None
        assert design2.include is None
    
    def test_split_by(self):

   223→

   229→        The experimental design
   230→    estimator : BaseEstimator
   231→        Scikit-learn estimator instance

   235→        Additional parameters

   239→                 estimator: BaseEstimator, model_type: Optional[str] = None, 
   240→                 **kwargs):

   245→            else:
   246→                model_type = "regression"
   247→        
   248→        super().__init__(dataset, design, model_type, **kwargs)

   256→        return self

   259→        """Make predictions using the sklearn model."""
   260→        if not self._is_fitted:
   261→            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
   262→        return self.estimator.predict(X)
   263→    
   264→    def predict_proba(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
   265→        """Predict class probabilities (classification only)."""
   266→        if not self._is_fitted:
   267→            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
   268→        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "predict_proba"):
   269→            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support predict_proba")
   270→        return self.estimator.predict_proba(X)
   271→    
   272→    def decision_function(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
   273→        """Get decision function values (classification only)."""
   274→        if not self._is_fitted:
   275→            raise ValueError("Model must be fitted before making predictions")
   276→        if not hasattr(self.estimator, "decision_function"):
   277→            raise AttributeError(f"{self.estimator.__class__.__name__} does not support decision_function")
   278→        return self.estimator.decision_function(X)
   279→    
   280→    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
   281→        """Get parameters including sklearn estimator params."""
   282→        params = super().get_params(deep)
   283→        if hasattr(self.estimator, "get_params"):
   284→            estimator_params = self.estimator.get_params(deep)
   285→            params.update({f"estimator__{k}": v for k, v in estimator_params.items()})
   286→        return params
   287→    
   288→    def __repr__(self):
   289→        """String representation."""
   290→        return (f"SklearnModelWrapper\n"
   291→                f"  Estimator: {self.estimator.__class__.__name__}\n"
   292→                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
   293→                f"  Fitted: {self._is_fitted}")

"""MVPA Model base class and model registry.

This module provides the base MVPAModel class and a registry system
for registering custom models.
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from typing import Dict, Type, Any, Optional, Union, Tuple
import numpy as np
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator

from .dataset import MVPADataset
from .design import MVPADesign


# Global model registry
_MODEL_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Type['MVPAModel']] = {}


def register_model(name: str):
    """Decorator to register a model class.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Name to register the model under
        
    Returns
    -------
    callable
        Decorator function
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> @register_model("my_classifier")
    ... class MyClassifier(MVPAModel):
    ...     pass
    """
    def decorator(cls: Type['MVPAModel']):
        _MODEL_REGISTRY[name] = cls
        return cls
    return decorator


def get_model(name: str) -> Type['MVPAModel']:
    """Get a registered model class by name.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Model name
        
    Returns
    -------
    Type[MVPAModel]
        The model class
        
    Raises
    ------
    KeyError
        If model not found
    """
    if name not in _MODEL_REGISTRY:
        raise KeyError(f"Model '{name}' not found. Available models: {list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())}")
    return _MODEL_REGISTRY[name]


def list_models() -> list:
    """List all registered models.
    
    Returns
    -------
    list
        List of registered model names
    """
    return list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())


class MVPAModel(ABC):
    """Abstract base class for MVPA models.
    
    This class defines the interface that all MVPA models must implement.
    It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_model.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset containing neuroimaging data
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model ('classification' or 'regression')
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional model-specific parameters
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model
    params : dict
        Model parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign, 
                 model_type: str = "classification", **kwargs):
        self.dataset = dataset
        self.design = design
        self.model_type = model_type
        self.params = kwargs
        
        # Validate model type
        if model_type not in ["classification", "regression"]:
            raise ValueError(f"model_type must be 'classification' or 'regression', got {model_type}")
        
        # Check compatibility
        if model_type == "classification" and not design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'classification' but design appears to be regression")
        elif model_type == "regression" and design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'regression' but design appears to be classification")
    
    @abstractmethod
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Fit the model to training data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Training data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray
            Training labels/values
        **fit_params : dict
            Additional fitting parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
            The fitted model
        """
        pass
    
    @abstractmethod
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions on new data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        X : np.ndarray
            Test data (n_samples, n_features)
            
        Returns
        -------

"""MVPA Model base class and model registry.

This module provides the base MVPAModel class and a registry system
for registering custom models.
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from typing import Dict, Type, Any, Optional, Union, Tuple
import numpy as np
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator

from .dataset import MVPADataset
from .design import MVPADesign


# Global model registry
_MODEL_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Type['MVPAModel']] = {}


def register_model(name: str):
    """Decorator to register a model class.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Name to register the model under
        
    Returns
    -------
    callable
        Decorator function
        
    Examples
    --------
    >>> @register_model("my_classifier")
    ... class MyClassifier(MVPAModel):
    ...     pass
    """
    def decorator(cls: Type['MVPAModel']):
        _MODEL_REGISTRY[name] = cls
        return cls
    return decorator


def get_model(name: str) -> Type['MVPAModel']:
    """Get a registered model class by name.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    name : str
        Model name
        
    Returns
    -------
    Type[MVPAModel]
        The model class
        
    Raises
    ------
    KeyError
        If model not found
    """
    if name not in _MODEL_REGISTRY:
        raise KeyError(f"Model '{name}' not found. Available models: {list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())}")
    return _MODEL_REGISTRY[name]


def list_models() -> list:
    """List all registered models.
    
    Returns
    -------
    list
        List of registered model names
    """
    return list(_MODEL_REGISTRY.keys())


class MVPAModel(ABC):
    """Abstract base class for MVPA models.
    
    This class defines the interface that all MVPA models must implement.
    It is the Python equivalent of R's mvpa_model.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset containing neuroimaging data
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model ('classification' or 'regression')
    **kwargs : dict
        Additional model-specific parameters
        
    Attributes
    ----------
    dataset : MVPADataset
        The dataset
    design : MVPADesign
        The experimental design
    model_type : str
        Type of model
    params : dict
        Model parameters
    """
    
    def __init__(self, dataset: MVPADataset, design: MVPADesign, 
                 model_type: str = "classification", **kwargs):
        self.dataset = dataset
        self.design = design
        self.model_type = model_type
        self.params = kwargs
        
        # Validate model type
        if model_type not in ["classification", "regression"]:
            raise ValueError(f"model_type must be 'classification' or 'regression', got {model_type}")
        
        # Check compatibility
        if model_type == "classification" and not design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'classification' but design appears to be regression")
        elif model_type == "regression" and design.is_classification:
            raise ValueError("Model type is 'regression' but design appears to be classification")
    
    @abstractmethod
    def fit(self, X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, **fit_params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Fit the model to training data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------

            Training data (n_samples, n_features)
        y : np.ndarray
            Training labels/values
        **fit_params : dict
            Additional fitting parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        self
            The fitted model
        """
        pass
    
    @abstractmethod
    def predict(self, X: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
        """Make predictions on new data.
        
        Parameters
        ----------

        """
        self.fit(X_train, y_train)
        return self.predict(X_test)
    
    def get_params(self, deep: bool = True) -> dict:
        """Get model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        deep : bool
            Whether to return nested parameters
            
        Returns
        -------
        dict
            Model parameters
        """
        return self.params.copy()
    
    def set_params(self, **params) -> 'MVPAModel':
        """Set model parameters.
        
        Parameters
        ----------
        **params : dict
            Parameters to set
            
        Returns
        -------

        """
        self.params.update(params)
        return self
    
    def __repr__(self):
        """String representation."""
        return (f"{self.__class__.__name__}\n"
                f"  Type: {self.model_type}\n"
                f"  Parameters: {self.params}")

"""Searchlight functionality for neuroimaging analysis.

This module provides searchlight iterators for analyzing local neighborhoods
of voxels within brain masks. Includes exhaustive, random, and clustered
searchlight implementations.

Direct translation of R's neuroim2 searchlight functions.
"""

import numpy as np
from typing import Iterator, Union, List, Optional
from .neuro_vol import NeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol
from .neuro_space import NeuroSpace
from .roi import ROIVolWindow, ROIVol, spherical_roi, ROICoords
from .utils import LazyList


def searchlight(mask: Union[NeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol], radius: float, 
                eager: bool = False, nonzero: bool = False, 
                cores: int = 0) -> Union[LazyList, List[ROIVolWindow]]:
    """Create an exhaustive searchlight iterator.
    
    This function generates an exhaustive searchlight iterator that returns
    ROIVolWindow objects for each searchlight sphere within the provided mask.
    The iterator visits every non-zero voxel in the mask as a potential center voxel.
    
    Parameters
    ----------
    mask : NeuroVol or LogicalNeuroVol
        A NeuroVol object representing the brain mask
    radius : float
        The radius (in mm) of the spherical searchlight
    eager : bool, optional
        Whether to eagerly compute the searchlight ROIs. Default is False,
        which uses lazy evaluation
    nonzero : bool, optional
        Whether to include only coordinates with nonzero values in the
        supplied mask. Default is False
    cores : int, optional
        Number of cores to use for parallel computation. Default is 0,
        which uses a single core. (Currently not implemented)
        
    Returns
    -------
    LazyList or list of ROIVolWindow
        A deferred_list object containing ROIVolWindow objects, each
        representing a searchlight region
        
    R Equivalent
    ------------
    neuroim2::searchlight
    """
    # Convert to LogicalNeuroVol if needed
    if not isinstance(mask, LogicalNeuroVol):
        mask = mask.as_logical()
    
    # Get indices to iterate over
    if nonzero:
        # Only iterate over nonzero voxels
        indices = np.where(mask.data.ravel())[0]
    else:
        # Iterate over all voxels
        indices = np.arange(mask.data.size)
    
    def generate_searchlight(idx):
        """Generate a single searchlight ROI."""
        # Get the center voxel index (from the subset if nonzero=True)
        if nonzero:
            center_idx = indices[idx]
        else:
            center_idx = idx
            
        # Get grid coordinates of center
        center_grid = mask.space.index_to_grid(center_idx)
        if center_grid.ndim == 2:
            center_grid = center_grid[0]  # Extract first row for single index
        
        # Create spherical ROI around this center
        roi = spherical_roi(mask, center_grid, radius, 
                           fill=1.0, nonzero=True)
        
        # Create ROIVolWindow with parent tracking
        return ROIVolWindow(
            data=roi.data,
            space=roi.space, 
            coords=roi.coords,
            parent_index=center_idx,
            center_index=0  # Center is first in coords after sorting
        )
    
    if eager:
        # Eagerly compute all searchlights
        return [generate_searchlight(i) for i in range(len(indices))]
    else:
        # Return lazy list
        return LazyList(generate_searchlight, len(indices))


def searchlight_coords(mask: Union[NeuroVol, LogicalNeuroVol], radius: float,
                      nonzero: bool = False, cores: int = 0) -> LazyList:

"""Tests for searchlight functionality."""

import pytest
import numpy as np
from pyneuroim import (
    NeuroSpace, LogicalNeuroVol, DenseNeuroVol,
    searchlight, searchlight_coords, random_searchlight,
    bootstrap_searchlight, clustered_searchlight,
    ROIVolWindow, ROIVol
)
from pyneuroim.utils import LazyList


class TestSearchlight:
    """Test exhaustive searchlight function."""
    
    def setup_method(self):
        """Set up test data."""
        # Create a small test mask
        self.mask_data = np.zeros((10, 10, 10), dtype=bool)
        self.mask_data[3:7, 3:7, 3:7] = True  # 4x4x4 cube = 64 voxels
        self.space = NeuroSpace(dim=[10, 10, 10], spacing=[2, 2, 2])
        self.mask = LogicalNeuroVol(self.mask_data, self.space)
    
    def test_searchlight_lazy(self):
        """Test lazy searchlight generation."""
        sl = searchlight(self.mask, radius=4, eager=False, nonzero=True)
        
        # Should return LazyList
        assert isinstance(sl, LazyList)
        assert len(sl) == 64  # Number of nonzero voxels
        
        # Access first searchlight
        first_sl = sl[0]
        assert isinstance(first_sl, ROIVolWindow)
        assert first_sl.space == self.space
        assert first_sl.parent_index is not None
        assert first_sl.center_index == 0
        
        # Check that searchlight contains voxels
        assert len(first_sl.coords) > 0
        assert len(first_sl.data) == len(first_sl.coords)
    
    def test_searchlight_eager(self):
        """Test eager searchlight generation."""
        sl = searchlight(self.mask, radius=4, eager=True, nonzero=True)
        
        # Should return list
        assert isinstance(sl, list)
        assert len(sl) == 64  # Number of nonzero voxels
        
        # Check all searchlights
        for s in sl:
            assert isinstance(s, ROIVolWindow)
            assert s.space == self.space
            assert len(s.coords) > 0
    
    def test_searchlight_all_voxels(self):
        """Test searchlight with nonzero=False."""
        sl = searchlight(self.mask, radius=4, eager=False, nonzero=False)
        
        # Should include all voxels
        assert isinstance(sl, LazyList)
        assert len(sl) == 1000  # 10x10x10
        
        # Check a searchlight from outside the mask
        corner_sl = sl[0]  # First voxel at (0,0,0)
        assert isinstance(corner_sl, ROIVolWindow)
    
    def test_searchlight_large_radius(self):
        """Test searchlight with large radius."""
        sl = searchlight(self.mask, radius=20, eager=True, nonzero=True)
        
        # Large radius should include many voxels
        for s in sl:
            assert len(s.coords) > 10  # Should have many neighbors


class TestSearchlightCoords:
    """Test searchlight_coords function."""
    
    def setup_method(self):
        """Set up test data."""
        self.mask_data = np.zeros((8, 8, 8), dtype=bool)
        self.mask_data[2:6, 2:6, 2:6] = True
        self.space = NeuroSpace(dim=[8, 8, 8], spacing=[1, 1, 1])
        self.mask = LogicalNeuroVol(self.mask_data, self.space)
    
    def test_searchlight_coords_returns_coordinates(self):
        """Test that searchlight_coords returns coordinate matrices."""
        sl_coords = searchlight_coords(self.mask, radius=2, nonzero=True)
        
        # Should return LazyList
        assert isinstance(sl_coords, LazyList)
        assert len(sl_coords) == 64  # 4x4x4 nonzero voxels
        
        # Get first coordinate set
        first_coords = sl_coords[0]
        assert isinstance(first_coords, np.ndarray)
        assert first_coords.shape[1] == 3  # Should have 3 columns (i,j,k)

content.content:
1→#' Wrap output results
     2→#'
     3→#' This function wraps the output results of the performance matrix into a list
     4→#' of spatial objects (NeuroVol or NeuroSurface) for each column 
     5→#' in the performance matrix, and structures it as a searchlight_result.
     6→#'
     7→#' @keywords internal
     8→#' @param perf_mat A performance matrix (voxels/vertices x metrics) containing classifier results.
     9→#' @param dataset A dataset object containing the dataset information (including mask and type).
    10→#' @param ids An integer vector of voxel/vertex indices corresponding to the rows of `perf_mat`.
    11→#'   These are typically global indices into the mask space for volumetric data, or vertex numbers for surface data.
    12→#' @return A `searchlight_result` object containing
    13→#'   \itemize{
    14→#'     \item `results`: Named spatial maps for each metric.
    15→#'     \item `n_voxels`: Total number of voxels/vertices defined by the mask.
    16→#'     \item `active_voxels`: Number of voxels/vertices with results.
    17→#'     \item `metrics`: Character vector of metric names.
    18→#'   }
    19→wrap_out <- function(perf_mat, dataset, ids=NULL) {
    20→
    21→  # Removed the strict stop condition for null ids if perf_mat has rows, 
    22→  # as combine_randomized might pass null ids for a dense matrix.
    23→  
    24→  # Check for dimension mismatch only if ids are provided
    25→  if (!is.null(ids) && !is.null(perf_mat) && nrow(perf_mat) > 0 && (nrow(perf_mat) != length(ids))) {
    26→    stop("Number of rows in `perf_mat` must match the length of `ids` when ids are provided.")
    27→  }
    28→  if (is.null(perf_mat) || ncol(perf_mat) == 0) {
    29→      # If perf_mat is NULL or has no columns, return an empty structure but with metadata
    30→      n_voxels_in_mask_empty <- 0
    31→      if (!is.null(dataset$mask)) {
    32→        if (inherits(dataset$mask, c("NeuroVol", "NeuroVec")))
    33→          n_voxels_in_mask_empty <- prod(dim(dataset$mask))
    34→        else if (inherits(dataset$mask, "NeuroSurface"))
    35→          n_voxels_in_mask_empty <- neurosurf::nvertices(dataset$mask)
    36→        else if (is.numeric(dataset$mask) || is.logical(dataset$mask))
    37→          n_voxels_in_mask_empty <- length(dataset$mask)
    38→      }
    39→      return(structure(
    40→          list(results = list(), 
    41→               n_voxels = n_voxels_in_mask_empty, 
    42→               active_voxels = 0, 
    43→               metrics = character(0)),
    44→          class = c("searchlight_result", "list")
    45→      ))
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  output_maps <- list()
    49→  metric_names <- colnames(perf_mat)
    50→  
    51→  if (is.null(metric_names)) {
    52→    metric_names <- paste0("Metric", 1:ncol(perf_mat))
    53→  }
    54→  
    55→  for (i in 1:ncol(perf_mat)) {
    56→    metric_vector <- perf_mat[, i]
    57→    current_metric_name <- metric_names[i]
    58→
    59→    if (inherits(dataset, "mvpa_surface_dataset")) {
    60→      if (!requireNamespace("neurosurf", quietly = TRUE)) {
    61→        stop("Package 'neurosurf' is required to handle surface datasets.")
    62→      }
    63→      current_ids <- ids
    64→      if (is.null(current_ids)) {
    65→        # For surface, if ids is NULL, assume all vertices of the mask geometry
    66→        if (inherits(dataset$mask, "NeuroSurface")) {
    67→             current_ids <- seq_len(neurosurf::nodes(dataset$mask))
    68→        } else if (inherits(dataset$mask, "SurfaceGeometry")) {
    69→             current_ids <- seq_len(neurosurf::nodes(dataset$mask))
    70→        } else if (is.numeric(dataset$mask)) {
    71→            current_ids <- which(dataset$mask != 0)
    72→        } else {
    73→            stop("Cannot determine vertex indices for surface data when 'ids' is NULL and mask is not NeuroSurface/SurfaceGeometry.")
    74→        }
    75→        if (length(metric_vector) != length(current_ids)) {
    76→            stop(paste0("Length of metric_vector (", length(metric_vector), 
    77→                        ") does not match number of vertices (", length(current_ids), ") for dense surface map."))
    78→        }
    79→      }
    80→      output_maps[[current_metric_name]] <- neurosurf::NeuroSurface(
    81→        geometry = geometry(dataset$train_data), 
    82→        indices = current_ids, 
    83→        data = metric_vector
    84→      )
    85→    } else { # Assuming volumetric dataset
    86→      if (is.null(ids)) {
    87→        # Create a dense NeuroVol if ids is NULL
    88→        # Assume metric_vector length matches the number of voxels in the mask space
    89→        if (length(metric_vector) != prod(dim(neuroim2::space(dataset$mask)))) {
    90→            stop(paste0("Length of metric_vector (", length(metric_vector),
    91→                        ") does not match total voxels in mask space (", 
    92→                        prod(dim(neuroim2::space(dataset$mask))), ") for dense volumetric map when ids is NULL."))
    93→        }
    94→        # Reshape metric_vector to array and create NeuroVol
    95→        vol_array <- array(metric_vector, dim = dim(neuroim2::space(dataset$mask)))
    96→        output_maps[[current_metric_name]] <- neuroim2::NeuroVol(
    97→            data = vol_array,
    98→            space = neuroim2::space(dataset$mask) 
    99→            # Masking will be implicit if the space is from a masked NeuroVol
   100→            # Or, if dataset$mask is logical/numeric array, it could be applied: data = vol_array[dataset$mask]

   214→#' Combine standard classifier results

   216→#' This function combines the standard classifier results from a good results data frame

   219→#' @keywords internal

   223→#' @return A list containing the combined performance matrix and other information
   224→combine_standard <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results) {
   225→  # Add improved error handling with diagnostics
   226→  if (nrow(good_results) == 0) {
   227→    futile.logger::flog.error("No successful results to combine. Examining errors from bad results:")
   228→    
   229→    # Analyze bad results to provide better diagnostics
   230→    if (nrow(bad_results) > 0) {
   231→      # Group error messages and count occurrences
   232→      error_summary <- table(bad_results$error_message)
   233→      futile.logger::flog.error("Error summary from %d failed ROIs:", nrow(bad_results))
   234→      
   235→      for (i in seq_along(error_summary)) {
   236→        error_msg <- names(error_summary)[i]
   237→        count <- error_summary[i]
   238→        futile.logger::flog.error("  - %s: %d occurrences", error_msg, count)
   239→      }
   240→      
   241→      # Show a sample of the first few errors
   242→      sample_size <- min(5, nrow(bad_results))
   243→      futile.logger::flog.error("Sample of first %d errors:", sample_size)
   244→      for (i in 1:sample_size) {
   245→        futile.logger::flog.error("  ROI %s: %s", 
   246→                                 bad_results$id[i], 
   247→                                 bad_results$error_message[i])
   248→      }
   249→    } else {
   250→      futile.logger::flog.error("No error information available.")
   251→    }
   252→    
   253→    stop("No valid results for standard searchlight: all ROIs failed to process")
   254→  }
   255→  
   256→  result <- NULL
   257→
   258→
   259→  
   260→  # Proceed with combining results
   261→  tryCatch({
   262→    ind <- unlist(good_results$id)
   263→    perf_mat <- good_results %>% dplyr::select(performance) %>% (function(x) do.call(rbind, x[[1]]))
   264→    
   265→    has_results <- any(unlist(purrr::map(good_results$result, function(x) !is.null(x))))
   266→    ret <- wrap_out(perf_mat, model_spec$dataset, ind)
   267→    
   268→    if (has_results) {
   269→      # Get prob_observed values - these may be NULL for regression
   270→      pob_list <- good_results %>% 
   271→        dplyr::select(result) %>% 
   272→        pull(result) %>% 
   273→        purrr::map(~ prob_observed(.))
   274→      
   275→      # Only process pobserved if we have non-NULL values (i.e., classification results)
   276→      if (any(!sapply(pob_list, is.null))) {
   277→        # Filter out NULL values (from regression results)
   278→        pob_list <- pob_list[!sapply(pob_list, is.null)]
   279→        
   280→        if (length(pob_list) > 0) {
   281→          pobserved <- bind_cols(pob_list)
   282→          
   283→          # Create appropriate type of output based on dataset type
   284→          if (inherits(model_spec$dataset, "mvpa_surface_dataset")) {
   285→            # For surface data
   286→            pobserved <- neurosurf::NeuroSurfaceVector(
   287→              geometry = geometry(model_spec$dataset$train_data),
   288→              indices = seq_len(nrow(pobserved)),  # Or appropriate indices
   289→              mat = as.matrix(pobserved)
   290→            )
   291→          } else {
   292→            # For volume data
   293→            nc <- ncol(pobserved)
   294→            mask <- as.logical(model_spec$dataset$mask)
   295→            if (nrow(bad_results) > 0) {
   296→              bad_ind <- unlist(bad_results$id)
   297→              mask[bad_ind] <- FALSE
   298→            }
   299→            pobserved <- SparseNeuroVec(
   300→              as.matrix(pobserved), 
   301→              neuroim2::add_dim(neuroim2::space(model_spec$dataset$mask), ncol(pobserved)), 
   302→              mask=mask
   303→            )
   304→          }
   305→          
   306→          ret$pobserved <- pobserved
   307→        }
   308→      }
   309→    }
   310→    
   311→    return(ret)

   313→    futile.logger::flog.error("Error combining results: %s", e$message)
   314→    futile.logger::flog.debug("Error details: %s", e$call)
   315→    stop(paste("Failed to combine searchlight results:", e$message))
   316→  })
   317→}

400→#'
   401→#' @keywords internal
   402→#' @param model_spec A list containing the model specification.
   403→#' @param good_results A data frame containing the successful classifier results.
   404→#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the unsuccessful classifier results.
   405→#' @return A list containing the combined performance matrix along with other information from the dataset.
   406→combine_vector_rsa_standard <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results) {
   407→  ind <- unlist(good_results$id)
   408→  perf_mat <- good_results %>% dplyr::select(performance) %>% (function(x) do.call(rbind, x[[1]]))
   409→  score_mat <- data.frame(sim=rowMeans(perf_mat))
   410→  ret <- wrap_out(score_mat, model_spec$dataset, ind)
   411→  ret
   412→}
   413→
   414→#' Combine randomized classifier results
   415→#'
   416→#' This function combines the randomized classifier results from a good results data frame
   417→#' and normalizes the performance matrix by the number of instances for each voxel index.
   418→#'
   419→#' @keywords internal
   420→#' @param model_spec A list containing the model specification.
   421→#' @param good_results A data frame containing the successful classifier results.
   422→#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the unsuccessful classifier results.
   423→#' @return A list containing the combined and normalized performance matrix along with other information from the dataset.
   424→combine_randomized <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results=NULL) {
   425→  # Check if we have results
   426→  if (nrow(good_results) == 0 || length(good_results$performance) == 0) {
   427→    futile.logger::flog.error("No valid results for randomized searchlight")
   428→    stop("No valid results for randomized searchlight")
   429→  }
   430→  
   431→  all_ind <- sort(unlist(good_results$indices))
   432→  ind_count <- table(all_ind)
   433→  ind_set <- unique(all_ind)
   434→  
   435→  # Get the number of performance metrics
   436→  ncols <- length(good_results$performance[[1]])
   437→  
   438→  # Create sparse matrix to hold results
   439→  perf_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i=rep(ind_set, ncols), j=rep(1:ncols, each=length(ind_set)), 
   440→                                  x=rep(0, length(ind_set)*ncols), 
   441→                                  dims=c(length(model_spec$dataset$mask), ncols))
   442→  
   443→  # Process each result
   444→  for (i in 1:nrow(good_results)) {
   445→    ind <- good_results$indices[[i]]
   446→    if (!is.null(ind) && !is.null(good_results$performance[[i]])) {
   447→      # Use tryCatch to handle errors gracefully
   448→      tryCatch({
   449→        m <- kronecker(matrix(good_results$performance[[i]], 1, ncols), rep(1, length(ind)))
   450→        perf_mat[ind,] <- perf_mat[ind,] + m
   451→      }, error = function(e) {
   452→        futile.logger::flog.warn("Error processing result %d: %s", i, e$message)
   453→      })
   454→    }
   455→  }
   456→
   457→  # Normalize by the count of overlapping searchlights
   458→  perf_mat[ind_set,] <- sweep(perf_mat[ind_set,,drop=FALSE], 1, as.integer(ind_count), FUN="/")
   459→  
   460→  # Set column names from the performance metrics
   461→  colnames(perf_mat) <- names(good_results$performance[[1]])
   462→  
   463→  # Wrap and return results
   464→  ret <- wrap_out(perf_mat, model_spec$dataset)
   465→  ret
   466→}
   467→
   468→#' Pool classifier results
   469→#'
   470→#' This function pools classifier results collected over a set of overlapping indices.
   471→#'
   472→#' @keywords internal
   473→#' @param ... A variable list of data frames containing classifier results to be pooled.
   474→#' @return A list of merged classifier results.
   475→#' @noRd
   476→pool_results <- function(...) {
   477→  reslist <- list(...)
   478→  check <- sapply(reslist, function(res) inherits(res, "data.frame"))
   479→  assertthat::assert_that(all(check), msg="pool_results: all arguments must be of type 'data.frame'")
   480→  good_results <- do.call(rbind, reslist)
   481→
   482→  ## map every result to the set of indices in that set
   483→  indmap <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nrow(good_results), function(i) {
   484→    ind <- good_results$indices[[i]]
   485→    cbind(i, ind)
   486→  }))
   487→  
   488→  
   489→  respsets <- split(indmap[,1], indmap[,2])
   490→  
   491→  merged_results <- purrr::map(respsets, do_merge_results, good_results=good_results)
   492→
   493→  return(merged_results)
   494→}
   495→
   496→
   497→
   498→#' Merge searchlight results
   499→#'
   500→#' This function merges searchlight results, combining the first result with the rest of the results.
   501→#'
   502→#' @keywords internal
   503→#' @param r1 A list of indices representing the searchlight results to be merged.
   504→#' @param good_results A data frame containing the valid searchlight results.
   505→#' @return A combined searchlight result object.
   506→do_merge_results <- function(r1, good_results) {
   507→  if (length(r1) > 1) {
   508→    first <- r1[1]
   509→    rest <- r1[2:length(r1)]
   510→    z1 <- good_results$result[[first]]
   511→    z2 <- good_results$result[rest]
   512→    ff <- purrr::partial(merge_results, x=z1)
   513→    do.call(ff, z2)
   514→  } else {
   515→    good_results$result[[r1[1]]]
   516→  }
   517→}
   518→
   519→#' Combine randomized searchlight results by pooling
   520→#'
   521→#' This function combines randomized searchlight results by pooling the good results.
   522→#'
   523→#' @keywords internal
   524→#' @param model_spec An object specifying the model used in the searchlight analysis.
   525→#' @param good_results A data frame containing the valid searchlight results.
   526→#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the invalid searchlight results.
   527→#' @return An object containing the combined searchlight results.
   528→pool_randomized <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results) {
   529→  if (nrow(good_results) == 0) {
   530→    stop("searchlight: no searchlight samples produced valid results")
   531→  }
   532→  
   533→  
   534→  merged_results <- pool_results(good_results)
   535→  
   536→  # Get prob_observed values - these may be NULL for regression
   537→  pob_list <- merged_results %>% purrr::map(~ prob_observed(.))
   538→  
   539→  # Only process pobserved if we have non-NULL values (i.e., classification results)
   540→  pobserved <- NULL
   541→  if (any(!sapply(pob_list, is.null))) {
   542→    # Filter out NULL values (from regression results)
   543→    pob_list <- pob_list[!sapply(pob_list, is.null)]
   544→    
   545→    if (length(pob_list) > 0) {
   546→      pobserved <- bind_cols(pob_list)
   547→    }
   548→  }
   549→  
   550→  ind_set <- sort(unique(unlist(good_results$indices)))
   551→
   552→  all_ids <- which(model_spec$dataset$mask > 0)
   553→  ## if we did not get a result for all voxel ids returned results...
   554→  mask <- if (length(ind_set) != length(all_ids)) {
   555→    mask <- model_spec$dataset$mask
   556→    keep <- all_ids %in% ind_set
   557→    mask[all_ids[!keep]] <- 0
   558→    mask
   559→  } else {
   560→    model_spec$dataset$mask
   561→  }
   562→  
   563→  
   564→  # Only create SparseNeuroVec for pobserved if we have classification results
   565→  if (!is.null(pobserved)) {
   566→    pobserved <- SparseNeuroVec(as.matrix(pobserved), neuroim2::space(mask), mask=as.logical(mask))
   567→  }
   568→  
   569→  #perf_list <- furrr::future_map(merged_results, function(res) compute_performance(model_spec, res))
   570→  perf_list <- purrr::map(merged_results, function(res) compute_performance(model_spec, res))
   571→  
   572→  ncols <- length(perf_list[[1]])
   573→  pmat <- do.call(rbind, perf_list)
   574→  
   575→  perf_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i=rep(ind_set, ncols), j=rep(1:ncols, each=length(ind_set)), 
   576→                                   x=as.vector(pmat), dims=c(length(model_spec$dataset$mask), ncols))
   577→  
   578→  
   579→  colnames(perf_mat) <- names(perf_list[[1]])
   580→  ret <- wrap_out(perf_mat, model_spec$dataset, ids=NULL) 
   581→  
   582→  # Only add pobserved if it's not NULL (i.e., for classification results)
   583→  if (!is.null(pobserved)) {
   584→    ret$pobserved <- pobserved
   585→  }
   586→  
   587→  ret
   588→}

   590→#' Perform randomized searchlight analysis

   592→#' This function performs randomized searchlight analysis using a specified model, radius, and number of iterations.
   593→#' It can be customized with different MVPA functions, combiners, and permutation options.
   594→#'
   595→#' @keywords internal
   596→#' @param model_spec An object specifying the model to be used in the searchlight analysis.
   597→#' @param radius The radius of the searchlight sphere.
   598→#' @param niter The number of iterations for randomized searchlight.
   599→#' @param mvpa_fun The MVPA function to be used in the searchlight analysis (default is \code{mvpa_iterate}).

#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param model_spec A list containing the model specification.
#' @param good_results A data frame containing the successful classifier results.
#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the unsuccessful classifier results.
#' @return A list containing the combined performance matrix along with other information from the dataset.
combine_vector_rsa_standard <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results) {
  ind <- unlist(good_results$id)
  perf_mat <- good_results %>% dplyr::select(performance) %>% (function(x) do.call(rbind, x[[1]]))
  score_mat <- data.frame(sim=rowMeans(perf_mat))
  ret <- wrap_out(score_mat, model_spec$dataset, ind)
  ret
}

#' Combine randomized classifier results
#'
#' This function combines the randomized classifier results from a good results data frame
#' and normalizes the performance matrix by the number of instances for each voxel index.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param model_spec A list containing the model specification.
#' @param good_results A data frame containing the successful classifier results.
#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the unsuccessful classifier results.
#' @return A list containing the combined and normalized performance matrix along with other information from the dataset.
combine_randomized <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results=NULL) {
  # Check if we have results
  if (nrow(good_results) == 0 || length(good_results$performance) == 0) {
    futile.logger::flog.error("No valid results for randomized searchlight")
    stop("No valid results for randomized searchlight")
  }
  
  all_ind <- sort(unlist(good_results$indices))
  ind_count <- table(all_ind)
  ind_set <- unique(all_ind)
  
  # Get the number of performance metrics
  ncols <- length(good_results$performance[[1]])
  
  # Create sparse matrix to hold results
  perf_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i=rep(ind_set, ncols), j=rep(1:ncols, each=length(ind_set)), 
                                  x=rep(0, length(ind_set)*ncols), 
                                  dims=c(length(model_spec$dataset$mask), ncols))
  
  # Process each result
  for (i in 1:nrow(good_results)) {
    ind <- good_results$indices[[i]]
    if (!is.null(ind) && !is.null(good_results$performance[[i]])) {
      # Use tryCatch to handle errors gracefully
      tryCatch({
        m <- kronecker(matrix(good_results$performance[[i]], 1, ncols), rep(1, length(ind)))
        perf_mat[ind,] <- perf_mat[ind,] + m
      }, error = function(e) {
        futile.logger::flog.warn("Error processing result %d: %s", i, e$message)
      })
    }
  }

  # Normalize by the count of overlapping searchlights
  perf_mat[ind_set,] <- sweep(perf_mat[ind_set,,drop=FALSE], 1, as.integer(ind_count), FUN="/")
  
  # Set column names from the performance metrics
  colnames(perf_mat) <- names(good_results$performance[[1]])
  
  # Wrap and return results
  ret <- wrap_out(perf_mat, model_spec$dataset)
  ret
}

#' Pool classifier results
#'
#' This function pools classifier results collected over a set of overlapping indices.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param ... A variable list of data frames containing classifier results to be pooled.
#' @return A list of merged classifier results.
#' @noRd
pool_results <- function(...) {
  reslist <- list(...)
  check <- sapply(reslist, function(res) inherits(res, "data.frame"))
  assertthat::assert_that(all(check), msg="pool_results: all arguments must be of type 'data.frame'")
  good_results <- do.call(rbind, reslist)

  ## map every result to the set of indices in that set
  indmap <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:nrow(good_results), function(i) {
    ind <- good_results$indices[[i]]
    cbind(i, ind)
  }))
  
  
  respsets <- split(indmap[,1], indmap[,2])
  
  merged_results <- purrr::map(respsets, do_merge_results, good_results=good_results)

  return(merged_results)
}



#' Merge searchlight results
#'
#' This function merges searchlight results, combining the first result with the rest of the results.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param r1 A list of indices representing the searchlight results to be merged.
#' @param good_results A data frame containing the valid searchlight results.
#' @return A combined searchlight result object.
do_merge_results <- function(r1, good_results) {
  if (length(r1) > 1) {
    first <- r1[1]
    rest <- r1[2:length(r1)]
    z1 <- good_results$result[[first]]
    z2 <- good_results$result[rest]
    ff <- purrr::partial(merge_results, x=z1)
    do.call(ff, z2)
  } else {
    good_results$result[[r1[1]]]
  }
}

#' Combine randomized searchlight results by pooling
#'
#' This function combines randomized searchlight results by pooling the good results.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param model_spec An object specifying the model used in the searchlight analysis.
#' @param good_results A data frame containing the valid searchlight results.
#' @param bad_results A data frame containing the invalid searchlight results.
#' @return An object containing the combined searchlight results.
pool_randomized <- function(model_spec, good_results, bad_results) {
  if (nrow(good_results) == 0) {
    stop("searchlight: no searchlight samples produced valid results")
  }
  
  
  merged_results <- pool_results(good_results)
  
  # Get prob_observed values - these may be NULL for regression
  pob_list <- merged_results %>% purrr::map(~ prob_observed(.))
  
  # Only process pobserved if we have non-NULL values (i.e., classification results)
  pobserved <- NULL
  if (any(!sapply(pob_list, is.null))) {
    # Filter out NULL values (from regression results)
    pob_list <- pob_list[!sapply(pob_list, is.null)]
    
    if (length(pob_list) > 0) {
      pobserved <- bind_cols(pob_list)
    }
  }
  
  ind_set <- sort(unique(unlist(good_results$indices)))

  all_ids <- which(model_spec$dataset$mask > 0)
  ## if we did not get a result for all voxel ids returned results...
  mask <- if (length(ind_set) != length(all_ids)) {
    mask <- model_spec$dataset$mask
    keep <- all_ids %in% ind_set
    mask[all_ids[!keep]] <- 0
    mask
  } else {
    model_spec$dataset$mask
  }
  
  
  # Only create SparseNeuroVec for pobserved if we have classification results
  if (!is.null(pobserved)) {
    pobserved <- SparseNeuroVec(as.matrix(pobserved), neuroim2::space(mask), mask=as.logical(mask))
  }
  
  #perf_list <- furrr::future_map(merged_results, function(res) compute_performance(model_spec, res))
  perf_list <- purrr::map(merged_results, function(res) compute_performance(model_spec, res))
  
  ncols <- length(perf_list[[1]])
  pmat <- do.call(rbind, perf_list)
  
  perf_mat <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i=rep(ind_set, ncols), j=rep(1:ncols, each=length(ind_set)), 
                                   x=as.vector(pmat), dims=c(length(model_spec$dataset$mask), ncols))
  
  
  colnames(perf_mat) <- names(perf_list[[1]])
  ret <- wrap_out(perf_mat, model_spec$dataset, ids=NULL) 
  
  # Only add pobserved if it's not NULL (i.e., for classification results)
  if (!is.null(pobserved)) {
    ret$pobserved <- pobserved
  }
  
  ret
}

#' Perform randomized searchlight analysis
#'
#' This function performs randomized searchlight analysis using a specified model, radius, and number of iterations.
#' It can be customized with different MVPA functions, combiners, and permutation options.
#'
#' @keywords internal
#' @param model_spec An object specifying the model to be used in the searchlight analysis.
#' @param radius The radius of the searchlight sphere.
#' @param niter The number of iterations for randomized searchlight.
#' @param mvpa_fun The MVPA function to be used in the searchlight analysis (default is \code{mvpa_iterate}).
